inscription.head:
No. 294.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1933-34.)
KEṄGOṆḌA, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription which refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jōyidēvarasa as ruling over
Beḷuhuge Seventy and Banavāsi Twelve-thousand is dated in the Chālukya-Vikrama
year 102, Viḷambi, Puśhya śu. 3, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti. The date is irregular.
In Viḷaṁbi neither śu. 3 nor the Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkramaṇa fell on Sunday. In the
previous year, however, (i.e., in Hēmaḷambi) both these did occur on Sunday. So if
the latter is the intended date, its equivalent would be A.D. 1177, December 25,
Sunday. The year 102 is the latest known year of the Chālukya-Vikrama era.

It records the gift of land made by Dāsa-gāvuṇḍa of Honuṅgali or Honugali and
his son Hariyamma for the worship and offerings of the god Mūlasthāna-Mallikārjuna
and for effecting repairs to the temple. The gift was entrusted to Kariya Dharmarāśi-
Paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple. It also records gifts of tolls, etc., made by
Suṅkada Gāvuṇḍa-Sāvi and others.

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(B.K. No. 54 of 1932-33.)
HARAḶAHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up against the front wall of the house of Honnappa Byāḍagi.

inscription.p:
This inscription referring to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vikramāditya as ruling Banavāse-
nāḍu from his capital Guttavoḷal is dated in Śaka 1110, Plavaṅga, Phālguṇa, śu. 13,
Thursday corresponding to A.D. 1188 February 11, Thursday, f.d.t. ·61. It
states that Daṇḍanāyaka Dāsirāja, the mahāpradhāna of Vīra-Vikramāditya constructed
the temple of Dāsēśvara in the vicinity of the temple of Sōmēśvara at Goṭṭagaḍi. It
also registers the gift of the village Kōdaḷiya Hosavūr in Perbālu Seventy made by
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mācharasa. It further registers a gift by Dāsirāja, of the village
koṁṇaji in Noḷambavāḍi, which was formerly given to him by Jagadēva-Pāṇḍya at the
time of his installation (Paṭṭabandhōtsava). The grant of the village Kuṁchigere (?)
by Sōmaladēvi and others at the time of the Paṭṭabanḍhōtsava of Vijayā-Pāṇḍyadēva,
son of Oḍeyarasadēva is also recorded. The recipient of the gifts was Kalyāṇaśaktidēva
of the Kāḷāmukha sect.

© svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ ·ujjaya-

padapayōdharānaṁditamayūraṁ māninīmānasarbbasvasūṟekāṟaṁ raṇadoḷnīṟaṁ paṁ
nirbbaragaṁḍaṁ samaraśauṁḍaṁ viṣamaturaṁgamārōhaṇādityaṁ vīravikra-

mādityaṁ banavāsenāḍaṁ duṣṭanigraha śiṣṭapratipāḷanadiṁ pāḷisuttaṁ guttavoḷala nele
vīḍinoḷpukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttamire || tatpāda-

padmōpajīvi śrīmanmahāpradhānaṁ niravadhi niraṁtaradānasanmāna prabhumaṁtrōtpāha
śaktiyuktaṁ gurupadapayōjaparimaḷāsaktaṁ pa-

tihitabrati kuśāgriyasūkṣmamati haracaraṇaparijātiśayabāṇaṁ sakaḷakaḷāpravīṇaṁ
śaraṇāgatapayōnidhi brāhmatējōnidhi mā-

hēśvaragaṇāgragaṇyaṁ niraṁtarapuṇyaṁ maṁtraśaktipradarśitatṛtīyanētraṁ gautamagōtra
pavitraṁ vaṁdisaṁdōhakalpabhūjaṁ śrīmaddāsirājaṁ nijapatiprājyarā-

jyabhāradhaurēyanāgi sukhadinirpanātananvayaprasiddhiyeṁtene || dhareyoḷcelvāda noḷpiṁdame
kṛtayugabījaṁ varaśrīsarōjaṁ taraṇiprakhyātatējaṁ

sphuritaguṇasamājaṁ dvijāṁbhōjarājaṁ varabhōgōddēvarājaṁ sadaudāryya
ramābhṛtkalpabhūjaṁ sthiratege girirājaṁ diṭaṁ mācirā-

jaṁ || ·ā mācirājanaṁgane bhūmiya dhairyyake ratiya calvigā śrīrāmeya maysiri
giṁbenipā mallauvveya caritramalte pavitraṁ || janiyi-

sidanava[riṁ] tānudayananudayananudayagiriyoḷudayisi sakaḷāvanipūjyanāgi budha
sajjanamudamaṁ paḍedu paḍeda teṟadiṁ meredaṁ ||

samanādaḷpaṁpe paṁpāsarada sirige dalpaḍi nīvṛtiyiṁ tīrtthamenippācāradiṁ nirmmaḷa
teyinudayādityarājatkarasparśamahāsaṁtuṣṭiyiṁ jīvanaba-

huḷateyiṁ rāja[vaṁ]sakramaprōdyamadiṁ gāṁbhīryyadiṁ śrīyagalada neleyiṁ sānu
kūlatvadiṁ tāṁ || gaṁgeya tāyaguṇpu vinutēśvaranaṁbeya

puṇyaśaktināgāṁganeyavveyaggaḷike sōmananiṁbine pettaḷoḷpuvettaṁgada soṁpu malla
ṇiya mātṛvadanyate dāsirājanaṁ maṁgaḷapūrṇnanaṁ besale-

yādaḷa paṁpeya peṁpu cōdyamē || [·āmardudu]sucittalōkāgama samucita
śaktyupāya maṁtrade mantryuttamaṁ sōmaṁgēkamukhaṁ dvimukha tri-

mukhaṁ caturmukhaṁ paṁcamukhaṁ || śṛtiyaṁ rakṣippa lōkaṁ [ta]ḷeva dhareyanu[dhdhā]
ripuddaṁḍa hṛ[dbhiṁnate]māḷpanyōrvviyaṁ kāḷvesapa surabhiyoḷpa parōdyaddaśā-

sya kṣitidōrppī bhūmi saṁklēśamanaleva subuddhatvadindirppa kāḷēyateyaṁ māṇippa
viṣṇvatyavataraṇa (©) ramādhāmanī maṁtri sōmaṁ || ·āta-

na tammaṁ vibudhabrātaṁ taṁmaṁ samaṁtu porevanenalvikhyātilatetāragegaḷapūtaṁmaṁ
dāsirājanesedaṁ nisadaṁ || kavitage sōltu jāṇnuḍige mecci suvṛ-

ttige kūrttu tanna bāndhavakanurāgamaṁ taḷedu nalmege tāgisi dharmmakāryyadoḷtavikisi
rājakāryyabharadoḷ dṛḍhanāgi virōdhivarggadoḷjavanavolā-

gi rājisuvanārppaḍi perggaḍe dāsirājanoḷ || vadanaṁ vāgnṛtyaraṁgaṁ hṛdayavabhavanā
sthānaraṁgaṁ viḷāsaṁ madanaśrīcāturaṁgaṁ sucaritatatigaṁgātaraṁgaṁ sudharmmā-

bhyudayaṁ tatvāṁtaraṁgaṁ bujabaḷamarivadhyāṁśaraṁgaṁ samanādudu maṁtraṁ dāsirā
jaṁgadhipasugṛharakṣākavāṭōttaraṁgaṁ || savanenipaṁnarār śivane meccuva na-

ccina bhakti sāmimeccuva padasuprasiddhi guru meccuva saccaritaṁ budhāḷi meccuva
caturōkti vīrati meccava niccaṭagūrppu kānte meccuva ratavoppalī ja-

game meccuvareccuva dāsirājanoḷ || paḍedartthaṁ kanyakāsaṁnibhavene kuḍalāyitu
dharmmānurāgaṁ kaḍunaṁpuḷḷākevōlmā-

ṇade neneyipudāyīśvarārādhanaṁ taṁnoḍa[leṁ]taṁtōvalāytāḷdana sukha
vasuveṁtaṁte dal rakṣyamāyte[ṁ]doḍarippaṁ dāsirājaṁ bagemige puruṣārttham
jagattīrttha-

māgal || kulatanumatividyāśrībalasucaritasubhagatātiśayami[vu] tannoḷnelasi nile
dāsirājaṁ nalivaṁ kalyāṇaśaktimunipana da-

yeyiṁ || ·ā munīśvarana gurukulaprabhāvamentene || dhṛtatanu dhātu kiṭṭamiḷiyaltapa
dūṣmege kiṭṭagāvesantatiyadu parbbatāvaḷiyenippudu ni-

ścaḷavṛttiyiṁ niyāmita niyamōtthakāḷamukhadiṁdame kāḷamukhatvamoppe śaktyatiśaya
śaktiparṣeyamṛtēśamunīśanoḷoppi tōṟuguṁ ||

thana śiṣyaṁ manmathana geldu tapadoḷaṁ rūpinoḷaṁ hṛnmukhadoḷirisi śivanasanmati sarbbēśvara
bratīśaṁ meṟedaṁ tībratapaḥśikhivṛddhyōdayārasātiśaya-

mētadaticitraṁ sarbbēśvarayōgīśē kalau kṣamārūpake nityaṁ || vidviṣōpi manōbhava
pramathanō nīrāgacittōpyasau bhaktābhīṣṭaphalapradaḥ sukhayutō-

pyudyattapaḥklēśitaṁ | nirdvaṁdyōpi viruddhavṛttakamidaṁ sarbbēśvarō naiṣṭhikavyāsā
rōpi sarasvatīpriyatamō bhāti kṣamāsaṁstutaḥ || sirigīśaṁ vāṇigīśaṁ

tapada mahimegīśaṁ lasatkīrtigīśaṁ haribhakti prītigīśaṁ yativitatigadhīśaṁ mahā
tārkkikēśaṁ gurumeccalbhaktigīśaṁ caturavitati-

gīśaṁ janakkellamīśaṁ varamaṁ pūṇdīva peṁpiṁdese[dagu]ṇade sarbbēśayōgīśanāthaṁ ||
tatthiṣyaṁ meṟedaṁ rājattivapadapadmabhavanenipāyuṣmatāṁ bhavaca-

ritaṁ śrīmatśucikalyāṇaśaktiyatipatitiḷakaṁ || ·anituntīrtthōdakaṁ śrīpada jaladoḷe
meydōṟuguṁ rudralōkaṁ tanaguḷḷā rudraroḷpaṁ nilisuvuda-

du vapu śrīyoḷiṁpiṁdavā vāgvanitākāśaviśēṣaṁ nelasi rasanādēśadoḷ pōlvarāreṁde
nalī kalyāṇaśakti bratitatipati peṁpiṁ karaṁ soṁpuvettaṁ || ·uragaṁ mu-

ṭṭadu bādhemuṭṭadaḷideṁduṁ muṭṭadā vyādhiyubbaraveṁduṁ tanuviṁge puṭṭadenesuṁ
kalyāṇaśakti bratīśvaranaṁ sanmanadiṁdamormme nenedaṁgeṁdaṁdu nōḷpaṁdu
taccaraṇā-

bbakkeṟapaṁdu biccaḷisuvaṁdappoḷpanēneṁbudō || nalidellelliddanarkkuṁ puruṣamṛgamuva
ntalli tīrtthaṁ banaṁ dēgulamuṁteṁtappuvāvaṁgeṟapuda-

[dhā]tūttama[tvaṁ] || taḷameytarppa(ṁ)nnegantuḷkidanaṁde mige rāgābdhiyaṁ kayyoḷaṁ
kāloḷamuṁtī kaṁpideṁtādudo kivivugutappāgamārtthaṁ-

gaḷaṁ kaṁṇoḷe kāṇbaṁ niścayaṁ ka[ṇkivige] nimirddavuntēke gaurīvaraṁ hṛṁniḷayaṁ
kalyāṇaśaktibratigacaḷateyuṁteṁtenalcelvuvettaṁ || śrutadēvīvārddhakaṁ piṁgitu vaca-

na sudhāsvādadi kālakālēyata[rā |] lēpacaritrāmaḷasurasaridaṁbhaḥ pravāhaṁga
ḷiṁdōḍitu śaṁbhuśrīpādārādhananidhi mige maṁṇēṟi kalyā-

ṇaśaktibratitējōdīpavarttisthitiyinakhiḷa sādhyaṁdalāytēnaneṁbeṁ || dhātriyoḷidēṁ
dalmuktikṣētramenuttosedu goṭṭagaḍiyoḷsaccāritraṁ tanmuni-

yirppaṁ dhātranumiṁnaṟiyanadaṟa peṁpaṁ pogaḷal [||] ·ārāmādaḷisāramā • •
vipūratuṁgabhadrāsarittīraṁ dhānyasamṛddhibhāramatuḷaprāsāda nētrapri-

yākāraṁ taṁnolaviṁ svayaṁbhuvenisirdda sōmanāthōdayādhāraṁ kṣētramadā
munīṁdrasutapōvistāramēneṁbudō || sumanōratnakke niraṁjanamenuttire
niraṁ-

janaṁ ślāghyamiṁtī rameyendintirppaḷintīrameyenutire yāvarttakāvarttakam
puṇyamayaṁ tānādudiṁdiṁtene tereteresuttirpudantasthamaṁ saukhyamanyarnnō-

ḍalāgeṁdene mige meṟeguṁ tuṁgabhadrāviḷāsaṁ || tuṁgabhavābdiyī sthaḷadoḷirddara
permmege tānadoṁde goṭṭaṁgaḍiyappudeṁdu śivagaṁgeya sajjala-

pānamoṁde goṭṭaṁgaḍi muktigeṁdu paramēśvaranillige taṁnaneydegoṭṭaṁgaḍiveṁdu
goṭṭagaḍi sāṟuvudā pesaraṁ niraṁtaraṁ || gītaṁ gaṁdharvvaraṁ sōlise madhu
samayaṁ-

dōṟe puṟpāḷidīpaṁ jyōtirllōkakke tāṇaṁbaḍeye rucirakāḷāgurūddhūpa-
dhūmabrātaṁ mēghōpamaṁ bettire jalakaḷaśaṁ pēlivettaṁte bhṛṁgānvitaṁ
kaṇgoppe

sōmēśvarasukhabhavanaṁ saṁtataṁ celvudōrkkuṁ || karamesevā sōmēśvarapura-
doḷguru pēḷa līḷeyiṁdaṁ dāsēśvaramaṁ māḍisidaṁ dāsirājanatiśayavicitrapatra-

kramamaṁ || taradiṁ nōḷpara tōrppa mārppoḷepu jīvaṁboyda rūvāramāca
raṇāghātada pātamaṁ pratikṛtivyūhaṁgaḷiṁ sōvabhāsuraraṁgaṁ madamattacittagaja-

varggāḷānasustaṁbhavīpariyeṁbaṁtire tōrppa kaṁbameseguṁ dāsēśvarāvāsadoḷ ||
varadāsēśvaramaṁ munipara maṭhatatiyaṁ tadīya bhōjanagṛhamaṁ karamose-

du māḍisida dāsirājanaṁ pogaḷva mānavaṁ kṛtakṛtyaṁ || ·aṁtumāḍisi śakavarṣa
1110 neya plavaṁgasaṁvatsarada phālguṇa śuddha 13 [bṛ]haspativāradaṁdā dā-

sēśvaradēvaraṁgabhōga raṁgabhōga khaṁḍasphuṭita jīrṇnōddārakke dāsirājadaṁḍa-
nāyakaṁ vikramādityadēvaṁge pādapūjeyaṁ koṭṭu dhārāpūrvvakaṁ paḍedu
koṭṭa sthaḷavāvuvendaḍe

hoṁnavattiyalu hiriya keṟeya modalēriya ·āḍakedōṁṭamaranaynūṟūriṁ mūḍalu
beḷvoḷaladāriyiṁ baḍaga lōkasamudradiṁ mūḍalere mattareṁṭu

cāmēśvaradiṁ mūḍalu gadde mattaroṁdu mattamādēvatāmahige mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ
mācarasaṁ perbbāḷeḷpattaṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ kōdaḷiya hosavūraṁ śrī
madrājaguru

kalyāṇaśaktidēvara kālaṁ karcci dhārāpūrbbakaṁ sarbbābhyantaraviśuddhavāgi sarbbana
masyaṁ koṭṭanā vūra sīme mūḍalu navilahāḷu ·āgnēyadal beṁṇacikal teṁkalu

layakoḷa nairityaṁ kancagāṟagoḷaṁ paḍuval kaḷavara [bāgu] baḍagal piriyahaḷḷaṁ
mattaṁ jagadēvapāṁḍyaṁ taṁna paṭṭabaṁdhōtsavadūḷā dāsirājaṁge noḷa[ṁ*]ba
vāḍi-

bāḍaṁ koṁṇajiya [koṭṭoḍā]yūraṁ dēvarggeṁdā kalyāṇaśaktidēvara kālaṁ
karcci sarvvanamanyavāgi koṭṭaṁ [||*] ma © ttamoḍeyarasadēvana kumāraṁ
vijayapāṁḍyedēvana paṭṭa-

baṁdhōtsavado[ḷkuṁcigeṟeya] sōmaladēvi dēvarasa [ku]ṁpiseṭi pramukhaparivāraṁ
kalyāṇaśaktidēvara kālaṁ karcci dhārāpūrvvaka sarvvanamasyaṁ koṭṭar ||

svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harētsavasundharā[ṁ]ṣaṣṭhirvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ
jāyatē krimiḥ || © © © © śrī © sōmanātha śaraṇu ©

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(B.K. No. 55 of 1932-33.)
HARAḶAHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up against the front wall of the house of Honnappa Byāḍagi.

inscription.p:
This composite record contains two grants made on different dates. The first
one, made by Jōyidēva (II) jointly with his minister Susaṅga refers itself to the reign
of the Kalachurya king Āhavamalla and is dated in Śaka 1104, Plava, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṁkrānti. The Śaka year cited is the current year. In the cyclic year Plava,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti fell on Pus11hya ba. 2 Friday, corresponding to A.D., 1181
December 25, Friday. The grant was made for the temple of god Vikramēśvara
constructed by Jēyidēva (II) at Goṭṭagaḍi on the bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā. It is
stated that this grant was caused to be engraved on a copper plate at the instance of
Mallugi-Daṇḍanāyaka. who was the Dēśādhikāri. This record apparently is a copy
of the copper plate referred to above.

The second grant made by Dāsirāja, the minister of Vikramāditya II, is dated
in Śaka 1110, Plavaṅga, Phālguṇa śu. 13, Thursday, corresponding to A.D., 1188
February 11, Thursday f.d.t. ·61. The Śaka year cited is the current year. It is
stated that Dāsirāja got a piece of land from Vikramāditya (II) and granted it for
the feeding of the ascetics, etc. Record is also made of the grant made by
Vikramāditya himself for the merit of his mother Padmaladēvi. The recipient of
the gifts was Rājaguru Kalyāṇaśakti of the Kāḷāmukha sect and Kiṭṭagāve-santati.
The genealogy of Kalyaṇās11akti as well as of Vikramaditya is given. Vīra-
Pāṇḍyadēva and Kumāra Vīra-Pāṇḍyadēva are mentioned.

l.ka:

ṭānvitā || yasya śrīpadapa-

dmavāsabhavanē yā saṁsthitā śrīvadhū tatprītyā vinatassarōjanayanōyastaṁ
nutapradyutidugdhā-

bdhau sudaśāvatārakṛtivadbhātyunmukhē tanmukhē pāyātsa stutavikramēśagiriśaḥ kalyāṇa
śaktiṁ sadā || śrīvarano-

lduraṁgabaliyaṁ padaraṁgadoḷāgumāḍe nētrāvṛtarōciyiṁ padanakhapratibiṁbitarūpa
śōbhe sadbhāvade vāmanaṁbolire bhūvadhuvettanadāvaniṁdavaṁdāvibhu vikramēśanolaviṁ

paripāḷike vikramārkkanaṁ || bhuvanacaturddaśaṁ giriśanājñeyinudbhavipaṁdu māḍi
bhūbhuvanamadentenalpeṟavu lōkada laukikarilli dharmmakarmmavanesagalbaḷikkesagidaṁtuṭa
neydu-

vareṁdoḍintu vaṁdyane divameṁbinammeṟegumurbbareyāgadāgaḷuṁ || bhavanaṁ [nī]
vaḷisalbaṁṇavuraṁdīvidaḷo ś®ṣṭiyene kanakanagēṁdrave dīpaṁ dvīpābdhigaḷave va-

rṇnakramada vaḷayamenaleḷeyeseguṁ || navalāvaṇyavanēnittave bhūvadhuguḷida jaḷadhiyāṟuṁ
pēḷī lavaṇāṁbhōnidhivōlenisuvudaṁtassaradhiyādīparidhi beḍaṁgiṁ ||

kosedeṁnaṁ nōḷke dēvāsuratatimakaraṁ kaṁtulakṣmakke nirmmānaseyarnnārāyaṇōra
sthaḷake jaḷagajaṁ dēvanāthaṁge śaṁkhaprasaraṁ śrīviṣṇuhastakkodavida nore dēvabrajāsvā
danakkeṁde sa-

maṁtīyirddaveṁbaṁntesedu lavaṇavārāsi bhōreṁbudiṁbiṁ || ·aṁbudhi tiḷigoḷanadaṟo
ḷjaṁbūdvīpame sarōjamagakuḷadaḍamā jaṁbūnadādrikarṇnikeyeṁbinavāytu
sirige meysi-

riyiṁdaṁ || kṣitibhṛtpatiyiṁdamiḷāvṛtadiṁ kiṁpuruṣadiṁde harivarṣadinūrjjita himagiri
yiṁ teṁkalnutabharatakṣētramadaṟoḷāryyākhaṁḍaṁ || ·āryyākhaṁḍadoḷatyāśa(śca ?)
ryya[ṁ]

śrīmajjanātiramyaṁ sumanaḥparyyaṁtritameseguṁ bhūnāryyuttamadēśamenipa kuṁtaḷa
dēśaṁ || kuṁtaḷadēśadoḷmisupa kīrtiyehūheṟeyāge madhyasīmaṁta sumārgga
māge nijadigvi-

jayōdgativikramaprabhāvaṁ tanigeṁpu soṁpeseva seṁduradaṁdamadāge nōḍa bhūkāṁtage
meccuvettamogaveccamanāgisidā caḷukyarōḷ || naliyuttirdduṁ dhātrīlalane
baḷikkavaranuḷidu kū-

rttirddaḷ bijjaladēvanoḷativīryyaṁ salisuva basanakke basanigeyarārkkūrar ||
ravibiṁbaṁ vairivīrarggakhiḷadigadhipasthānamā ·ōṭaviṭṭarggavaśēṣa[ṁ] parbbataṁgaḷvara
nṛpasatiyarggabdhigaḷtadgaḷa • • • • • pūrakurbbarāgahvaramaribaḷa-

kāgalke taddēvatākṣōbhavadātmakṣōbhadiṁ muṁparidudenisidaṁ bijjalakṣōṇipāḷaṁ ||
ātatakīrttiyākaḷacurikṣitipammeṟediṁ baḷikkamaṁtātana taṁmanī dharaṇiyaṁ vibhu
mayḷugidēvanāḷidaṁ khyā-

tiyenippa darppaṇadoḷeyde janaṁgaḷa daṁtakāṁti saṁghātamupāśrayaṁ baḍeye varṁṇisa
varṁṇada varṁṇaśōbheyiṁ || jagatīvaranādaṁ mayḷugidēvana marmmanenipa
karṁṇanṛpāḷaṁ dviguṇise vīramanahitartri-

guṇipa biṁnaṇadiniṟidu guṇiyipa gaṇakaṁ || ·ākarṁṇana kiṟiyayyaṁ bhūkāṁtākarṁṇa
pūramaṁ samedaṁ tīkṣṇākarṁṇapūrṁṇaśarapātāka[rṣita] śatruśīrṣadiṁ sōma
nṛpaṁ || kurukutsage purūravaṁge sagaraṁgā kārttavī-

ryyaṁge bittarisirddādinaḷaṁge rājipa hariścaṁdraṁge vikhyātiyiṁ siriyiṁ sāgaramaṁ
vivarddhisuva guṇpiṁ dōrbbaḷavyāptiyiṁ turagaprauḍhiyinārppiniṁ doreyenippaṁ
sōmabhūmīśvaraṁ || rāyamurāri sōmadharaṇī-

patiyiṁ baḷikaṁ mahītaḷaśrīyoḍanirddanātananujaṁ vibhu mallugidēvananma
bhūjāyege nāṇaputtu molagarttaleyāvaṭavirppa bhaṁgi saṁdāyatavāge rājaguṇa
moppire taṁnoḷudāttavṛtti-

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1932-33.)
GUTTAL, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in Survey No. 664.

inscription.p:
This inscription referring itself to the rule of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Vikramāditya
over Banavase-nāḍu, is dated in Śaka 1112, Saumya, Āśhāḍha śu. 2, Vaḍḍavāra,
corresponding to A.D., 1189 June 17, Saturday. The Śaka year cited is the current
year. After giving the genealogy of the Gutta chiefs upto Vikramāditya (II),
it states that he built a temple called Padmēśvara for the merit of Padmaladēvi who
was his nurse (dhātrī). It also records a gift of land made for the same temple,
at the time of the installation of the deity by Vikramāditya. The recipient of the gift
was Rājaguru Kalyāṇaśakti, whose genealogy is also given. Guttavoḷal is mentioned
as the capital of Vikramāditya. The inscription is much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1932-33.)
GUTTAL, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone lying in front of the temple of Chennakēśava

inscription.p:
This badly damaged inscription refers itself to the rule of a Gutta chief (name
lost) and is dated in Śaka [1]114, Virōdhikṛit, Monday. Other details are lost.
The Śaka year cited corresponds to A.D. 1191-92. On the strength of the date the
record may be ascribed to Vikramāditya II. It seems to record the death of
Bamaya-Nāyaka in a fight. Guttavoḷal is mentioned.

l.ka:
nibaruṁ gutta[voḷala] • • • • • jjeya ba[la]yanāyakara magaṁ vīrabamayanā
yakaṁ • • • • • hō-

• • • • • vidhivatsudruṣṭaṁ || jitēna labhyatē lakṣmīrmritēnāpi surāṁganā
kṣaṇa-
vidhvaṁsini kāyē kā ciṁtā maraṇē raṇē || śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 299.
CHAUḌADĀNPUR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Muktēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription is a composite record of two grants of land and refers to the
rule of Āhavāditya Vikramāditya over Banavāse-nāḍu. Of the two grants, one made by
Khaṇḍeyakāṟa Kāmeya, the subordinate of Vikramāditya, is dated in Śaka 1113,
Virōdhikṛit, Mārgaśira Amāvāsyā, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1191 December
18. The week-day was Wednesday. The gift, meant for the worship and offerings
of god Muktēśvara and for the repairs to the temple, was entrusted to Muktajīya.

The second grant, made by Vikramāditya himself, in the presence of the
mahājanas of Gōpe was made a month later, i.e., Virōdhikṛit, Puśhya Amāvāsyā, Thurs-
day [Saṁkramaṇa], corresponding to A.D. 1192 January 16, Thursday. The saṁkra-
maṇa, however, did not occur on that day.

The record gives the genealogy of Vikramāditya and states that Vikramāditya of
Ujjayini, whose descendants the Guttas claim to be, had acquired aśhṭamgāsiddhi. The
genealogy of Kāmeya is also given.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1932-33.)
HARAḶAHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up against the front wall of the house of Honnappa Byaḍagi.

inscription.p:
This inscription referring itself to the rule of Āhavāditya Vīra-Vikramāditya over
Banavāsi Twelve-thousand is dated in Śaka 1136, Śrīmukha, Chaitra Amāvāsyā, Monday,
solar eclipse, Saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1213 April 22, Monday. There was a
solar eclipse on that day but no Saṁkrānti. The Śaka year cited is the current year.
The record which gives the detailed geneology of the Gutta family, says that
Vikramāditya II had a sister called Vijayamahādēvi who was married to Siṅgi-
nṛipāḷa of Sāntaḷi-maṇḍala and whose son Ballāḷa married Tuḷuvaladēvi, the daughter
of Vikramāditya II. It states that Vikramāditya constructed the temple of
Tuḷuvalēśvara in the vicinity of the temple of Svayambhu-Sōmanāthadēva at Goṭṭagaḍi
on the western bank of the Tuṅgabhadrā, in memory of his daughter Tuḷuvaladēvi
who died after giving birth to a son called Bamma. Further it records the
grant of the village Guḍḍahāḷapaḷḷi for the worship and offerings to that deity. The
recepient of the gift was the Rājaguru Trilōchana-Paṇḍita, the sthānāchārya of the
god Sōmanātha.

manmahāpradhānaṁ caṁdeyaṇadaṇnayakarssahitavirddu dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi koṭṭaru |
gutta-

voḷala sthaḷavrittihiriyakeṟeya keḷage kuṁbhēśvarada gaddeyiṁ teṁka vikkamēśvarada gadde ·alliṁ
mūḍalu

tuḷuvalēśvarada gaddeyarevattalu | kaṁmatadiṁ mūḍalu ·ere mattalu ·āṟu 6 || ·ā
grāma-

da sīmeyeṁteṁdaḍe mūḍalu hi[ri]da hāḷa paḍuvaṇavōṇi mēre ·āgnēyadalu kaṟalamaḍu

ttada bāgu nairityadalu ·eranakoḷa pacchimadalu bīranakoḷa hoṁnāvareya bāgu

vāyāvyadalu naṭṭakallu baḍagalu tuṁgabhadrā || hulluṇiya hāhanūra talevolanalliheṁmā
ḍaya ba[ṁ]-

manakōḍiṁ baḍagaltuḷuvaladēviyargge puṇyārtthavāgi brahmapurige biṭṭa vitti hadinaidakke
matta 60 ||

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(B.K. No. 135 of 1932-33.)
KANAVAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into a wall inside the temple of Durgā.

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Vikramāditya as ruling over
Banavāse-nāḍu and is dated in the year 1155 of Chālukya-chakravarti Sōyidēva,
Vijaya, Chaitra śu. As11hṭami, Monday. The year cited is apparently of the Śaka
era. But for the week-day, the details correspond to A.D. 1233, March 20, the
week-day, however, being Sunday and not Monday as recorded.

The Chālukya-Chakravarti Sōyidēva mentioned in the record may be the
Western Chālukya king Sōmēśvara IV. But the date given, viz., Śaka 1155 would
be too late for him, for his last date known so far is A.D. 1200. The period in
which this record was made, was one of confusion, the Chālukya kingdom having
been divided between the Yādavas and the Hoysaḷas who were fighting with each
other for supremacy. Apparently though no longer king, Sōmēśvara IV was still
being nominally acknowledged as ruler in some parts of the country.

It records a grant of land made by Vikramāditya and Chenna-gāvuṇḍa of
Kannavaḷḷi, for the worship and offerings to god Kallināthadēva of Mūlasthāna.

dēvadēvaṁ namastubhyaṁ sarvvajñassarvvatōmukhaḥ śrīkallināthabhūtēśaḥ traiḷōkyavara
dāyakaḥ ©

śivāya śāṁtarūpāyanaṁtarūpāya dhīmahē sarvvagīrvvāṇanāthāya kallinātha
namōstutē ©

kaḷa bhuvanamaṁ sakāruṇyadiṁ rakṣisuttamirddu || śrīkalidēvanī bhuvanavastuviśēṣamanī
viśālaratnākaramaṁ samaṁ-

nteseva kuṁtaḷa dēśamaniṁte nōḷpeneṁdā kanakādriyaṁ mudadinēṟidaneṁdaḍadēke baṇnipai
nākanivāsamaṁ sakaḷapuṇya-

phaḷāmritamippadēśamaṁ || ·āntādēvaṁ dēvalōkadiṁ bhaktajanasulabhanappudaṟiṁ bhūlōka
kkavatarsiyanēkātiśayamaṁ nōḍuvalli bhūkā-

[nte]yuṭṭa paṭṭāvaḷiyante matsyakūrmmagajanarāvaḷiyiṁ śōbhākaramāgi gaṁbhīra
nīrākaramirddudadeṁtene || vasudhākānteya lōcanadvaya-

vaktradaṁte sarōjāṁkitavāytu nōrppaḍenasuṁ ratnāgaraṁ sāgara || ·ā jaḷadhi baḷedu
baḷasida bhūjagamadhyapradēśadoḷu kanakanagaṁ rājīva-

rāgabhōga bhrājitamaṇimukuḷadaṁte kaṇgesedikkuṁ || ·antāśaiḷada śīlamumā giriya
gurutvamā gōtrada gōtratvamumā parvvata-

da sarvvasvamumippaṁtirddudā dvīpamadeṁtene || rajatādriyiṁde teṁkalu kujamikkuṁ
jaṁbuveṁbudā pesareṁdaṁdajaniḍalā

nijanāmaṁ trijagakkaṁ negaḷe negaḷda jaṁbūdvīpaṁ || ·ā jaṁbūdvīpadoḷanēka vastu
vistāradiṁ sogayisuva dēśamadāvudene ||

kantuharanennida bhavana kāṁteya kuṁtaḷada kusmakuḷamaṇi muktāsaṁtatiyantesedikkuṁ
kuṁtaḷadēśaṁ viśēṣa vastubrajadiṁ ||

·ā dēśadoḷsogayisuva banavasenāḍa calvadaṁtene || kūḍe surabhavanavudadhiyanēḍisuva
taṭākavamaravanamahimeyanaḷḷāḍisu-

va banaṁ banavasenāḍallada nāḍu nāḍeyuṁ sogeyiputē(dē) || ·ā nāḍoḷuḷidaveseyave
dhēnugaḷā kāmadhēnu suragiriyā

kānanave kalpabhūruhavēnaṁ pogaḷdapudā negaḷda beḷuhigenāḍaṁ || ·ā nāḍoḷpalavuṁ
teṟadineseva paṭṭaṇaprākāraveṁtene || vri || sutti

vi[dhaṁ] baluhappude durggavittalī guttoḷalattalā madhureyallade pālisaluṁṭe dhātri
yoḷu || kaṁ || ·ā durggā-

dhipanennidanādiya naḷanahuṣaninajanā dharmmajanabjōdarasutanene negaḷdaṁ sādhita dikcakra
vikramāṁkaṁ dhareyoḷu || praśa ||

svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ ·ujjēnīpuravarādhīśvaraṁ vaṭavrikṣa
lāṁchitavijayadhvajaṁ bhu-

nanditamayūraṁ māninīmānasarvvasvasūṟekāṟaṁ raṇadoḷu nī[ṟaṁ] pagemaccegaṁḍaṁ
samaraśauṁḍaṁ viṣamaturaṁgamā-

rōhaṇāditya vīravikramādityadēvaṁ banavāsenāḍaṁ duṣṭanigrahaśiṣṭapratipāḷaneyiṁ
rājyaṁgeyyuttamire || vri || pāvuge kaṁ-

the bettapaṭa kharpparavāghuṭikārasaṁ pogarttīvidudoṁdu kūrasi manaṁbagedallige
pōpa bēḷpa ratnāvaḷiyaṁ sacētana-

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(B.K. No. 91 of 1933-34.)
HONNATTI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Rāmaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the rule of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jōyidēva and is dated in
Plavaṁga Kārtika Śu. Pūrṇima, Monday. The Śaka year is not given; but the details
work out regularly to A.D. 1187, October 19, Monday, in the year Plavaṁga corres-
ponding to Śaka 1109.

It states that Sarvapradhāva Tipparasa constructed the temple of Rājēśvara in
the premises of the Chāmēśvara temple of Honnavatti at the instance of Sarvādhikāri
Bīchiseṭṭi, (who made certain gifts to that temple). It also registers grants of lands,
house-site and certain incomes, made by Hiriya Jōyidēva, Bīchiseṭṭi, Tipparasa and
other residents of Honnavatti (Modern Honnatti).

l.ka: vara pratiṣṭeya māḍidanu dēvarige staḷavriti bē-

l.note: This may be a short form of maṁḍaḷika

l.ka: tu kayi mane 1 dēvagoḷaga kaliseṭi muṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(B.K. No. 71 of 1932-33.)
HĀVANŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This badly mutilated inscription refers to Vikramāditya and to a Yādava king.
It appears to record a grant made by a certain Maiḷaladēvi, for the offerings to god
Bhillēśvara and for a charity water-shed.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(B.K. No. 101 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and undated inscription refers to Jōyidēva and seems to record
the death of a hero (name lost), probably the son of a certain Bōviya-Nāyaka, in a
fight. As no other details are available, it is difficult to identify this Jōyidēva.

edition.p: The writing is completely effaced and peeled off.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in a field-near the Doḍḍa-masūti.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 522, Kālayukta, Mārgaśira śu. 2 Monday.
Dr. Fleet who remarks that the date is not authentic gives the equivalent as
A.D. 600, November 13, which was a Sunday and not Monday.

This record which refers to Mahāsāmantādhipati Śāntivarma of the Māṭūra
family as governing Banavāsi Twelve-thousand with the (river) Varade as the boundary,
narrates a conversation with the maidens of Palarūr when Śāntivarma visited the
village and demanded grass for his horses and elephants.
This record is published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XI, pp. 1ff.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the temple of Banaśaṁkari.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record of about the 7-8th century A.D. seems to register a gift
of gōsāsa made by Ammakīrti.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(B.K. No. 1 of 1932-33.)
agaḍi, Haveri Taluk, Dharwar District.

p.i: stone standing in s. No. 155 near (the temple called) Chiṭlavvanaguḍi.

inscription.p:
this short inscription dated in śaka 700 corresponding to a.d. 778-79 records
that the figures of śaṅkhanidhi, srīyādēvi and padmanidhi were carved by mukundōvaja,
anumānakuśala and būdōvaja. the three figures are sculptured in relief above the
inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1932-33.)
Dēvagēri, Haveri Taluk, Dharwar District.

p.i: broken stone near the doḍḍa-masūti.

inscription.p:
this fragment of an inscription seems to record some grant made by mācha-
gāmuṇḍa and others when sēnamallara kaṇṇamma was the nāḻgāmuṇḍa of bāsavūr
hundred-and-forty. the record belongs to about the 8th century a.d.

l.note: Some lines before this have been broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1932-33.)
DĒVĪHOSŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in S. No. 393.

inscription.p:
This inscription in characters of about the 8th century A.D., states that when
Śrī-Māra was governing the nāḍu a hero named Gōvinda died in a cattle-raid.
The woman named Binnapōṭi erected the memorial stone which was engraved
by Guṇaga.

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1932-33.)
HĀVANŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in front of the temple of Honnēśvara.

inscription.p:
This fragment of a damaged record seems to record a gift of land to a deity.
Maḻāvaḷḷi (village ?) and Māda-gāmuṇḍa are mentioned. The characters are of
about the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(B.K. No. 98 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar near the temple of Rāmaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This inscription in early characters of about the 8th Century A.D. records a
gift of land and the erection of a mēṇṭi probably by Māramma.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1933-34.)
GUḌGŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in Survey No. 39.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription in characters of about the 8-9th century A.D. records
the death of a hero. The names Vāsa and Āneya are found.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the temple of Rāmaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This inscription of about the 8-9th century A.D. records the erection of a mēṇṭi
by Ponnamāra and another person. Siṅgeyara Vinakā (Vināyaka) made it.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the temple of Rāmaliṅga.

inscription.p:
This inscription written in characters of about the 8-9th century A.D. records a
gift of land and gōsāsa by Muḻkāḍara Mārapuli.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(B.K. No. 116 of 1933-34.)
RĀṆEBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying in front of the Veṅkaṭēśa Bank.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 781, corresponding to A.D. 859-60, records the
grant of land made to Nāguḷa-Basadi, constructed by Nāguḷara Pollabbe. The gift
was made in the presence of the Ūrgāmuṇḍas. The recipient of the gift was
Nāgaṇandyāchārya of Siṁghavura-gaṇa. The village Attīgēri is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(B.K. No. 23 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar near the temple of Banaśaṁkari.

inscription.p:
This broken record in characters of about the 9th century A.D., states that a
mēṇṭhi was made by Būdōvaja. Rājāditya is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1932-33.)
DIḌGŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab near the tuṁbu in S. No. 1.

inscription.p:
This record of about the 9th century A.D. states that when Kattiyara “was ruling
the earth”, Dōsi, who was governing Banavāsi Twelve-thousand remitted on the
occasion of an eclipse, a portion of the tax of Saṅgamūr, after washing the feet of the
mahājanas of Mugunda.
Published on p. 253 of Ep. Ind., Vol. VI.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(B.K. No. 33 of 1933-34.)
SIRAGUPPI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone leaning against the back wall of the temple of Kiṭṭada Hanumān.

inscription.p:
The purport of this damaged record is not clear. The names, Paraśurāma and
Indra are found. A certain Dōra seems to have erected the stone.
The characters are of about 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(B.K. No. 176 of 1932-33.)
YELLĀPUR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone on the tank bund.

inscription.p:
Only the imprecatory portion of a bigger record is preserved on this stone. It
is stated to have been composed by Konnayya. The copyist's name is lost. The
engraver's name is given as Nāgōja.
The characters are of about 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(B.K. No. 94 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Rāmalinga.

inscription.p:
This short inscription records the death of a hero named Balmaḻchha. The
characters are of about the 9th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1934-35.)
SIRUÑJA, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the village school.

inscription.p:
This damaged record in characters of about the 9th century A.D.
commemorates the death of a person (name lost) in a cattle-raid.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1932-33.)
HATTIMATTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in a lane near the Hoṇḍa.

inscription.p:
This hero-stone written in characters of about the 9-10th century A.D. is badly
damaged and worn out. It seems to record the death of a hero.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(B.K. No. 36 of 1934-35.)
IṬGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the Māruti temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record, written in characters of the 9-10th century A.D. mentions
Iṭṭige and seems to record a grant of land.

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(B.K. No. 109 of 1932-33.)
HOMMARAḌI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone leaning against the front wall of the temple of Vīrabhadra.

inscription.p:
This inscription states that when Pokkuḻi was governing Banavāsi Twelve-thousand
and Chelliga was the nāḻgāmuṇḍa a hero (name lost) died in a cattle-raid. Kasi-
[do]ṇḍara Śri (Si)riyamma is mentioned.
The record belongs to about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(B.K. No. 193 of 1932-33.)
KURHAṬṬI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a stone lying near the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record refers to the construction of a temple and a grant
made to it. It is engraved in characters of about the 10th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(B.K. No. 87 of 1933-34.)
GUḌGŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in Survey No. 39.

inscription.p:
This record in characters of about the 10th century A.D. mentions a certain
Rājasi[ṁga] as administering Punnamanti-nāḍu (modern Honnatti). It is badly
damaged and broken.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(B.K. No. 111 of 1933-34.)
MŌṬĒBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone lying in Mādaragēri.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record seems to register a grant of land made to a Jaina
basadi. Chandranandi-bhaṭṭāra, Indara-Piṭṭama and his Sēnabōva Kundamayya are
mentioned.
The characters belong to about the 10th century A.D.

l.ka: [sa] biṭṭudu vāraṇāsi sāsirvva pārvvaru sāsira kavi-
leyumankonda lōkakke [sandōnakkuṁ] yasya yasya yadā bhūmi tasya
tasya tadā phalaṁ svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasundha-

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(B.K. No. 162 of 1933-34.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone (A-39) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This hero-stone of about the 10th century A.D. commemorates the death of a hero
(name lost), who was the grandson of Lōkeya-Pergaḍe.

edition.p: 1. A few lines in the beginning are completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1933-34.)
GAAGANĀTH, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken stone built into the bund called sthāpane of the Varadā river in
front of the Gaḷagēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary and damaged record seems to describe the valour of a hero
(name lost). The divisions of Kadambaḷige and Gaṅga-maṇḍala are mentioned.
The characters belong to the 10-11th century A.D.

l.note: Some letters above this line are visible but no sense can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1933-34.)
HOSAHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in front of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This damaged hero-stone seems to record the death of Paḍevaḷa Eṟeyamma
and a grant of land made in his memory. The characters belong to the
10-11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(B.K. No. 140 of 1933-34.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone (A. 20) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 963, Visha (Vṛisha), Jyēśhṭha, ba. 8, Sunday,
records a grant of land by Boḷḷabbe, the daughter of Gommarasa, to a temple construc-
ted by her. The land was given to her to be used for charitable purposes by
Gommarasa. The gift was entrusted to Chandraśēkhara-Bhaṭṭāra. The Sēguṇa
family is mentioned.

The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1041, May 25, Monday (not Sunday
as stated in the record).

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(B.K. No. 113 of 1932-33.)
HOMMARAḌI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone leaning against the front wall of the temple of Rāmaliṅga.

inscription.p: This inscription is dated in Śaka 970 Sarvadhāri, corresponding to A.D. 1048-49.

It records a gift of income from betel-leaf garden made by Gālayya, to Duṇḍa Chaṭṭa
of Ommaṛaḍi (modern Hommaraḍi) who fought with and killed the daṇḍe (guard) of
the (guild of) Five-hundred-and-four (Eleya-Bojaṅgas) when the daṇḍe reviled this
body.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(B.K. No. 123 of 1932-33.)
KAḶḶĪHĀḶ, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in front of the temple of Dayā-Paramēśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 977, Manmatha, Chaitra ba. 11, Friday,
Vishu-saṁkrānti. The date is irregular. By the Saṁkrānti and week-day the details
correspond to A.D. 1055, March 24, Friday. The tithi however was 9 which ended
at ·58. It states that Suṁkavergaḍe Chaṭṭimayya, Manneya Rājādityarasa and others
made a gift of taxes for burning a perpetual lamp to god Sādēvēśvara.

It also contains another record of a grant of money for the same purpose made
on Pushya śu. 5, [Uttarāya]ṇa-saṁkrānti (apparently of the same year). The
week-day is lost. The details correspond to A.D. 1055, December 25, Monday; f.d.t. ·14.
The donor of the latter grant was Kaliyammarasa of the Jīmūtavāhana lineage and
Khachara family.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(B.K. No. 12 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Two pillars set up in front of the temple of Yellamma.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 987, Viśvāvasu, Bhādrapada śu. Shashṭi, Monday.
The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1065, August 10, Wednesday (not Monday).

It registers a grant of land made by Mahāsāmanta Rājādityarasa of the Jīmūta-
vāhaṇa lineage and Khachara family to the god Gōyindēśvaradēva. The gift was
entrusted to Gaṅgarāśi-Munipa. Rājāditya is stated to be holding the manneya of
Bāsavūra One-hundred-and-forty.

l.ka: śāsaspadanaṁ vivēkanidhi rājādityanaṁ baṇniku || kaṁ || lalane-
rttilalane kaḍu[gāḷe] naguvaḷu jalanidhiyaṁ dāṁṭi sakaḷadi-
ge negardda rājādityaṁ (||) kālaṁ kaḻci dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi gōyi-

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1934-35.)
IṬGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Piece of broken slab near the Virūpakshaliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription is dated in Śaka 991, Saumya, Chaitra Amāvāsya,
Thursday, Saṁkramaṇa. The details are irregular. The tithi cited corresponds to
A.D. 1069, March 25, Wednesday. The saṁkramaṇa occurred on Tuesday the 24th
March.

It states that a Mahāsāmanta (name lost) who was in charge of the Manneya of
Raṭṭapaḷḷi Seventy and Iṭṭage Thirty, which together constituted the Nūṟuṁbāḍa,
made a grant of land for the worship and offerings to god Virūpākshadēva of Iṭṭage.

The record was written by Sandhivigrahi Masaṇayya and was engraved by
Saṁkōja.

l.ka: dēvargge snāna nivēdyakke dēguladiṁ teṁka viriyiṁ mūḍa
beḻdale mattarondu kannaragaṭṭada keḷage gaḻde kamma ·aydu

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(B.K. No. 57 of 1933-34.)
KŌṆANATAṀBIGE, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone near a well on the bank of the Varada river.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka [9]95, Pramādīcha, Vaiśākha pūrṇima, lunar
eclipse, Saṁkrānti corresponding regularly to A.D. 1073, April 24, Wednesday.

It records a grant of land for worship and offerings to god Saṁkēśvaradēva by
Saṁka-gāvuṇḍa who built the temple. He is stated to have belonged to the
Kauśika-kula. The gift was entrusted to Chandrābharaṇa-Paṇḍita.
Kuḷakaraṇa Nāgadēva is stated to have written the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1932-33.)
DĒVANGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the temple of Mallikārjuna.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1[00]2, Raudri, Āśvayuja ba. 5, Sunday, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1080, October 5, Monday f.d.t. ·54.

It describes the valour of Kaleya Nāyaka of Dēvaṁgēri who fought for Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Jōyimarasa of the Jimūtavāhana lineage and Khachara family, when the
latter attacked Guttaḷal. A grant of land is stated to have been made in commemo-
ration of the hero.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1932-33.)
HATTĪMATTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab near the temple of Kōḍi-Honnavva.

inscription.p:
This record in characters of about the 11th century, A.D., registers a gift of
oil and paddy to the god Bhōgēśvaradēva by Telliga Jayasiṁgayyaseṭṭi and others
of the community of ayvattokkalu.

l.note: The word holliseṭṭi is written below the line with the mark.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(B.K. No. 148 of 1932-33.)
KŌḶŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab in front of the temple of Basavēs11vara.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record registers a grant in memory of a hero (name not clear).
Basava and Kētamalla are stated to be holding the office of Gāvuṁḍu. It was written
by Māḷapayya, who was the teacher of Kētamalla. The characters are of about the
11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(B.K. No. 180 of 1932-33.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Slab built into the ceiling of the left maṇḍapa of the eastern gōpura of
the Vīranārāyaṇasvāmi temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record of about the 11th century A.D. seems to record a grant of
and made by Bāyikabbe to Chidaṇṇa. The grant of bhaṭṭa-vṛitti and chhātra-vṛitti
are also recorded.

l.ka: • yana cidaṇnaṁge goṭṭakeṟeya keḷa • • matta 3 mattaṁ ta •

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1933-34.)
KĀGINELLI, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up at the back of the temple of Kāḷahastīśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the death of Buliga in Eḷevāḍi (?). The characters are
of about the 11th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1933-34.)
BAMMANAKAṬṬI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in Survey No. 122.

inscription.p:
This inscription states that Kēta, the son-in-law or nephew of Nāgaseṭṭi, con-
structed a tank to the west of Negeyūr. It is also recorded that he was pierced to
death by the thieves and that his mother Mākabbe and his son Hanuma erected the
stone. The characters are of about the 11th century A.D.

l.ka: ḷpaṁ jagamella hogaḻuttire mige negaḻdaṁ nāgaseṭṭiyaṟiya kētaṁ

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1934-35.)
YALISĪRŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the village chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This damaged stone of about the 11th century A.D. seems to record the death of
a hero, in the cyclie year Sarvajit. Polega and Āyicha are stated to have erected the
memorial slab.

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(B.K. No. 190 of 1932-33.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the back wall of the temple of Hanumān at the Koneri-tīrtha.

inscription.p:
This fargmentary record contains an culogy of Lokkiguṇḍī and its one-thousand
Mahājanas. It is written in characters of about the 11-12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(B.K. No. 28 of 1933-34.)
KĀGINELLI, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Sculptured pillars built into the walls of the omb of Śrī Bhaṇḍārakēri-
svāmi in front of the shrine of Ādikēśava.

inscription.p:
The record A mentions a sculptor Komma, the disciple of Chāva. B and C
describe the valour of Ādityavarma with his titles Dānavinōda and Vairi-Nārāyana.
The remaining one (comprising D and E) records the setting up of a pillar in a basadi
by Masaṇa. The characters are of about 11-12th century A.D.

edition.h4: Pillar bulit into the right wall.

edition.h4: Pillar built into the left wall.
Pillar built into the back wall.

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1934-35.)
UKKUND, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone near the Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record mentions a member (name lost) of Māṭūra family
who is described as the lord of Trikundapura. It seems to record a grant of land.
The characters belong to about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(B.K. No. 37 of 1932-33.)
DĒVĪHOSŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone in the temple of Bhōgēśvara.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated in Śaka 1082, Vikrama, lunar eclipse, Monday.
In the given year there were two lunar eclipses, one in Bhādrapada and the other in
Phālguṇa, corresponding respectively to A.D. 1160, August 18 and A.D. 1161,
February 12. But neither of them occurred on Monday. The first was on Thursday
and the second on Sunday. The latter being nearer to the week-day given in the
record, it may be surmised that the grant was made actually on Sunday, though it
was recorded on Monday.

It states that the one-thousand Mahājanas of Deyvada-Hosavūr (modern Dēvī-
hosūr) made a grant of land to the god Mūlasthāna-Bhōgēśvara (of that place). The
gift was entrusted to Sōvarāsiguru of Posavūr. Mahāpradhāna Hegaḍe Bammarasa
is extolled and a chief of the Jīmūtavāhana lineage is stated to be holding the
manneya of Bāsvaūra One-hundred-and-forty.

banavasenāḍadarkke tiḷa-

vetīpāta sōmavāradaṁdu

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(B.K. No. 108 of 1932-33.)
HIRĒMUGADŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the temple of Īśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka [10]87, Pārthiva, Mārgaśira Śu. Shashṭhi,
Friday, corresponding to A.D. 1165 November 11. The week-day was Thursday.

It states that on the above-mentioned date, Eṟabōva of Mugudūr died in a cattle-
raid. He is stated to be the servant of Eācha-gauṁḍa of Mugudūr.

l.ka: rggaśira suddha cchaṭṭi śukravāradaṁdu mugudūra • • • • • sū[ṟe]goṁḍu
tuṟuvariya hōhalli ·ā vūra ·eṟabōvaṁ taḷtiṟidu sattu suralōka pra-

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1933-34.)
GUḌADĀNVĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Beam on the top of the second entrance into the central shrine of the
Kallēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1104, Śubhakṛit, Mārgaśira śu. Pūrṇimā, Thursday
lunar eclipse. In the cyclic year given, there was no eclipse in the month of
Mārgaśira. But in the next year i.e., Śōbhakṛit (Śaka 1105), there was a lunar
eclipse on the given tithi and day. The details in the latter year correspond to
A.D. 1183, December 1, Thursday. This apparently is the date intended.

It records three grants made by three persons to god Mallikārjunadēva of Alūr
and god Yōgēśvaradēva of Brahmapuri. The first was made at the instance of
Daṇḍanāyaka Mallugidēva by Suṁkavergaḍe Nāgarasa and his younger brother
Dāvarasa, from the nelevīḍu of Hāvari. The second one was made by Suṁkavergaḍe
Sōvarasa, at the instance of Mahāmaṇḍales11vara Jōyidēvarasa. The donors of the
third were Lāḷakhaṇḍeyakārar-adhishṭhāyaka Vāsudēva-Daṇḍanāyaka and his
brothers Kēsava-Daṇḍanāyaka and Nāraṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka. Vāsudēva-Daṇḍanāyaka
held many other offices.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(B.K. No. 125 of 1933-34.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Second pillar at the south entrance to the Ark-killah.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1115, (current) Paridhāvi, Āshāḍha Pūrṇimā,
Saturday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṁkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1192, June 27, Saturday.
The saṁkrānti occurred on the previous Friday.

It records a grant of land for the worship and offerings of flowers to god
Narasiṁhadēva.

edition.h4: Text. Written in continuation of B.K. No. 124 of 1933-34.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1932-33.)
AKKŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of a broken image in the temple of Vīrabhadra.

inscription.p:
This inscription, bearing no date, describes in verse the deity s11antinātha and the
saints Sōmadēvakumāra and Sudhākaradēva belonging to Mūla-saṁgha. It also states
that a basadi was constructed by Sātōja-Rmsōja. The characters belong to the 12th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the front wall of the village gate.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription written in characters of about the 12th century A.D.,
records the death of a hero (name lost), while fighting with Siṁgaṇa-Dāṇḍānāyaka,
the adhikāri of Hāvri. Some grant seṇems to have been made in his memory. A certain
Mchidēvaē is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1932-33.)
DĒVĪHOSŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone built into the wall of the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

inscription.p:
This inscription of about the 12th century A.D., states that when
Mahāmaṇḍalēs11vara Kīrtidēvarasa attacked the agrahāra Deyvada-Posavūr, a
goldsmith named Dāsōja fought with him and died.

l.ka:
[ḷē]śvaraṁ kīrttidēvarasaniṟidu tuṟuva koṁḍu hōhalli | ·akkasāli dāsōjaṁ
māṟāṁtidircci kā[di]

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1932-33.)
DIḌGŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken slab in the temple of Hanumantadeva.

inscription.p:
This incomplete inscription describes the valour of Tailapadēva of the
Jīmūtavāhana lineage and Khachara family. The portion giving his genealogy is
broken off. It belongs to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1932-33.)
GAṆAJŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This inscription engraved in characters of the 12th century A.D., seems to record
the death of Asagara Kēta in a fight. Goyda-Gauḍa the prabhu of Gaṇajūr and
Tantrapāḷa Bammarasa are mentioned.

© halara koṁ[du] sattallige biṭṭa gaṟde ·asagara kētana gaddeyiṁ keḷage • •

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(B.K. No. 114 of 1932-33.)
HOSARITTI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone lying near the bhajanakaṭṭi.

inscription.p:
The purport of this fragmentary record is not clear. A certain Polega seems to
have been highly praised. The characters belong to about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(B.K. No. 150 of 1932-33.)
KUḶENŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Nandi-pillar near the tank bund.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record in characters of about the 12th century A.D., is in
Sanskrit language and mentions a certain Rēcharāja styled Amātya-śirōmaṇi.

edition.h4:
Text. At the top are engraved two lines which appear to form a separate
• • • • • rudra • • •
• • • • • nāykamaharē • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(B.K. No. 174 of 1932-33.)
YALAGACH, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Top of the entrance into the sanctuary of the temple of Veṅkaṭēśa.

inscription.p:
This inscription of about the 12th century A.D. mentions the son (name not
given) of Sānti-Seṭṭi. This seems to be just a portion of a record.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(B.K. No. 191 of 1932-33.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Panel of a sculptured Portico in the Trikūṭēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This small record in Sanskrit language describes the fame of Chandrabhūshaṇa-
Brati. It is engraved in characters of about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(B.K. No. 7 of 1933-34.)
BANKĀPUR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone built into the left wall of the southern entrance into the central
shrine of the Nagarēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This record contains two verses in praise of a hero called Rāmana-Siṅga. The
characters belong to about 12th century A.D.

l.ka: gūsinavolu jīvipa posadesege mige naguvanalte rāmanasiṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1933-34.)
BYĀDGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken hero-stone in the temple of Vīrabhadra.

inscription.p:
This inscription commemorates the death of Sōva-gauvuḍa, who died in a cattle-
raid at Bēḍage (modern Byāḍgi). The characters are of about the 12th century A.D.

l.ka: ralōrvvaṁ pa(ṁ)ṇḍitare pōhirellige gaṁṇḍa sōvaṇanu koṁda sama • • • • •
ṇadoḷu || pa || ·antu bēḍageyaṁ niṟidu tuṟuvaṁ koṁḍu hō • • •
• sōvagauvuḍaṁ vūrkkoṁḍidircci palabaraṁ koṁdu suralōka prāptanāda ||

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1933-34.)
KĀGINELLI, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up at the back of the temple of Kāḷahastīśvara.

inscription.p:
This fragment seems to record the death of a hero in a fight. The characters
belong to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(B.K. No. 146 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of stone (A-24) in the museum.

inscription.p:
In this fragment a king seems to have been referred to. The characters belong
to the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(B.K. No. 180 of 1933-34.)
KĀRJŌḶ, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the centre of the village.

inscription.p:
This inscription is very badly damaged. It seems to record a grant made
to the god Kēśavadēva. A certain Daṇḍanāyaka (name lost) and the village
Karaṁjōḷa are mentioned. It is written in characters of about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(B.K. No. 10 of 1934-35.)
BENKANKOṆḌA, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the eastern entrance into the Kalamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription, dated in Chaitra ba. Ashṭami, Bhāskaravāra (Sunday), records
the death of Māchiga, son of a certain Kēta. The characters belong to about the
12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(B.K. No. 30 of 1934-35.)
ITGI, RANEBENUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Door-frame at the first entrance into the Virūpākshaliṅga temple.

inscription.p:
This undated record states that when Garuḍa-Pāṇḍyadēva was governing
Nūrmubāḍa, his subordinate Heggaḍe Jannayya and Sātagauṁḍa and Kāḷagauṇḍa of
Iṭṭage made a grant of land for the worship and offerings to god Virūpākshadēva (of
Iṭṭage). The record is engraved in characters of the 12th century A.D.

māḍi biṭṭa ·ere ma-

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(B.K. No. 40 of 1934-35.)
KADARMAṆḌALGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Garuḍa pillar in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This badly damaged record mentions Kadimriḍi (the present Kadarmanḍalgi)
and seems to record a grant of land. The other details are not clear. It is engraved
in characters of about the 12th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1934-35.)
MĀKANŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Rāmēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the death of Dāsiga in a fight with Vijaya-Pāṇḍya and
states that Bhujabaḷa-Pāṇḍya rewarded Dāsiga and the valiant Eṟakama. It also
records a grant of land in Mākanūr, made by Chāmagaumḍa to both the heroes.

This record is dated in the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Bhādrapada śu. 13. The Śaka
year is not given. The characters belong to the 12th century A.D.

l.note: The letterra is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(B.K. No. 102 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in front of the Vīrabhadra temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription commemorating the death of Chikkagavuḍa, is dated
Parābhava, Kārtika ba• Pañchami, Monday. The Śaka year 11 • • is partly
preserved.

l.ka: © svasti śrīmatu sakavarṣa 11 • • parābhavasaṁvatsarada kārttika ba-

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the temple of Siddhēs11vara.

inscription.p:
This short inscription written in characters of about the 12-13th century A.D.,
states that Māaya, son of Kshēmaya-Nāyaka died in a fight at Banavase on Tuesday,
śu. 13, in the month of Bhādrapada of the cyclic year Virōdhi.

l.ka:
© śrī virōdhisaṁvatsarada bhādrapada su 13 maṁgaḷavāradalli kṣē[ma*]yenāykana
maga [mū]ḷaya banavaseya kaḷagadalli

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(B.K. No. 143 of 1932-33.)
KŌḶŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken hero-stone lying near the temple of Ḍoḷḷēśvara.

inscription.p:
This damaged record registers a grant of land made to Bīrabōva by Tribhuvana-
simgi and the Gauḍas of Kōḷūr. The characters are of about the 12-13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(B.K. No. 178 of 1932-33.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the southern gōpura of the Vīranārāyaṇasvāmi temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription of about the 12-13th century A.D., states that Mahāpradhāna
Mahadēva, a follower of the Jaina faith, renovated the Jina-bhavana. He is called
the pradhāna of Ekkala-bhūpāla of the town of Uddhare and Ekkaladēvana
rājyābhyudaya-karaṇa-kāraṇa (the cause of the rise of Ekkaladēva's kingdom).
The genealogy of Mahadēva is given.

hau[dā]-

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1933-34.)
HATTIMATTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Broken stone built into the wall of the backyard of the house of
Sōmangauḍa Dyāvanagauḍar.

inscription.p:
This inscription in characters of about the 12-13th century A.D. seems to
record a grant of land made by the potter Mādhava, in the presence of the Ūroḍeya
and Mahājanas of the agrahāra town Hattiyamattavūr.

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1934-35.)
BANNIHAṬṬI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Nandi-pillar behind the tēra-mane (car shed.)

The record being fragmentary, its purport is not clear. A certain [Mallikārjuna]
and Kōṭīśvara are mentioned. The characters belong to the 12-13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1934-35.)
MĀKANŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the compound of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the death by samādhi of Boppa-gauḍa of Mākanūr, the
disciple of Nandi-Bhaṭṭāraka of Mūlasaṁgha and Sūrastha-gaṇa. The inscription is
dated in the cyclic year Khara, Kārtika s11u. Friday. The characters belong to about
the 12-13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(B.K. No. 58 of 1932-33.)
HARAḶHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone on the river bank.

inscription.p:
This record dated in Śaka 11[29] (current), Kshaya, Kārtika, Pūrṇimā, Monday,
states that a hero named Kāḷeya died in a fight near Ānevari, with a certain Pāṇḍya,
while Rājaguru Kalyāṇas11aktidēva was sending his brother Dhruvēśvaradēva to
Śrīparvata. It is also stated that some land was granted in recognition of his
valour.

The tithi cited corresponds to A.D. 1206, October 18, Wednesday (not Monday as
stated in the record).

baḍiva mārvvaladoḍane taḷutiṟidu kādi halabaraṁ koṁdu

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(B.K. No. 132 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Sixth pillar in Ark-Killāh.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1162, Śārvari, Pushya Amāvāsyā, Sunday
corresponding to A.D. 1241, January 13, Sunday.

It records a grant of land and certain taxes by Sarvādhikāri Bhāgubāyi to the
worship and offerings of god Narasimhadēva of Vijayāpura.

l.ka: yakeya mattaru 3 nāvidigeyavara sthaḷada horeyalu matta-

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(B.K. No. 24 of 1934-35.)
HOḶI-ĀNAVĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the Saṁgamēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1168, Parābhava, Āśvija śu. 11, Monday, records
a gift of money for the offering of flowers to god Viśvarūpadēva. The donor was
Nāgavve, the mother of Sāteya-Nāyaka who bore the biruda Baḷḷeyar-Eṟeya.

The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1246, September 23, which was,
however Sunday, not Monday.

l.ka: kena tāyi nāgauve hūvina paḍige ko-

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(B.K. No. 117 of 1933-34.)
RĀNEBENNUR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the temple of Siddhēśvara.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in Śaka 1174, Paridhāvi, Vaiśākha ba • • • • •
(the tithi and week-day are lost), corresponding to A.D. 1252, April-May.
It mentions a Jaitugi. The characters are Nāgarī and the language is Marāṭhi.

l.de: svasti śrī saku 1174 paridhāvī saṁvachara vaiśākhu vadi • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(B.K. No. 43 of 1934-35.)
KARŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up near the temple of Kalamēśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1183 (Current) Raudri, Sravaṇa ba. 5,
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1260, July 29, Thursday.

It records a grant of land made for offerings to god Kalidēva and for conducting
the rite of Vasudhārā of the gods Vighnēśvara, Kshētrapāḷā and Nārāyaṇadēva. The
grant was made by Mahāpradhāna Vīriseṭṭi, son of Gaṇapiseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(B.K. No. 138 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone (A-18) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1200, Bahudhānya, Vais11ākha śu. 15, Sunday,
corresponding to A.D. 1278, May 8, Sunday.

It states that Sāyidēva-[Daṇḍanāyaka] a Brahmin of Vāsishṭha-gōtra constructed a
temple and installed therein the deities Rēchēśvara and Varadāni-Tripurushēśvara and
made a grant of land for the worship and offerings to the gods. Maṇḍaḷika
Mahadēva-Rāṇeya also made a grant of land to the same gods. The gift land was
purchased from Śivarās11idēva, the āchārya of the temple of Gōkēśvaradēva.

The first half of the record is in Sanskrit verse. A supplementary record on the
second face of the stone registers a grant for the resident priest and also enumerates
the qualifications of the person fit to perform worship.

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1933-34.)
KAḌKOḶ, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in front of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the death by the rite of samādhi, of Chandi-gauḍi, the
wife of Sāvanta Siriyama-gauḍa of Kaḍakoḷa. She was the lay disciple of Padumasēna-
Bhaṭṭāraka of Mūla-saṁgha. The date of her death is cited as Śaka 1201, Pramāthi,
Bhādrapada śu. Shashṭhi, Monday, corresponding to A.D. 1279, August 14, Monday,
f.d.t. 12.

A record of the grant of land made to a basadi by Hiriya-Boppa-gauḍa and others
is added at the end.

l.ka: tsarada bhādrapada suddha chaṭi sōmavāradaṁdu śrīma-
ya staṁbha(ḥ) maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī
ṭu samasta praje basadige koṭṭa yare mattaru 1 •

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(B.K. No. 100 of 1933-34.)
HŪLIHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Kallēs11vara.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record is dated in Śaka 1215, Vijaya, [Mārga]śira śu 5, Thursday,
corresponding to A.D. 1293, November 4. The week-day was Wednesday. The
following Thursday was a day of s11ūnya-tithi.

It registers a grant of land by Viṭharasa, the officer-in-charge of taxes, and others
for worship and offerings to a god (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone lying in the temple of Banas11aṅkari.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary stone of about the 13th century A.D. records the death of a,
certain Nāyaka (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(B.K. No. 50 of 1932-33.)
GUTTAL, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up on the tank bund.

inscription.p:
This inscription engraved in characters of about the 13th century A.D., records
the death of Holeya-Nāyaka, who fought with Baicheya and died in the presence of a
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvar (name doubtful). The title Ujeniya-Puravarādhīs11vara indicates
that the chief belonged to the family of the Guttas.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(B.K. No. 67 of 1932-33.)
HATTIMATTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in a lane near the Hoṇḍa.

inscription.p:
This record states that the hero-stone was set up by Maleya-Nāyaka in memory
of a hero, probably the servant of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mādarasa. The characters are
of about the 13th century A.D. Mādarasa is stated to be a devotee of Dhavaḷanātha
of Kālaḍi (cf. B.K. No. 70 of 1932-33).

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(B.K. No. 70 of 1932-33.)
HATTIMATTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Kōḍi Basappa.

inscription.p:
This damaged record states that Sāveya-Nāyaka, (son of) Chavuḍādēvi set up a
hero-stone in memory of a hero called Manaya-Nāyaka who died for his master
Chavuṁḍeya-Nāyaka of Mallūr. Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Mādarasa is mentioned. He
is stated to be a devotee of Dhavaḷanātha of Kālaḍi.
The characters are of about 13th century A.D.

l.ka: • ṭṭeyara mana[ya]nāyka mallūra cavuṁ[ḍe]ya nāya[ka*]na svāmi-

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(B.K. No. 83 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: North-west pillar in the navaraṅga in the Siddhēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This record belonging to the 13th century A.D. is dated in the cyclic year Sarvadhāri,
Kārtika Śu. 5, Thursday and states that, at the instance of Sarvādhikāri Vikadēvā-Paṇḍita,
Chavuḍarasa, the Mahājanas, Najaras, Ubhayamārga-Tuḷuvas and other bodies,
granted incomes on certain articles, for offerings to god Siddhanāthadēva (of Hāvēri).

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(B.K. No. 91-94 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Capitals of the pillars in the temple of Siddhēśvara.

inscription.p:
These inscriptions state that the respective pillars on which they are engraved
were caused to be made by Gaḷatigeya Malliseṭṭi and his three sons Karuva Siṁgiseṭṭi,
Mūkiseṭṭi and Mahadēvaseṭṭi. The characters belong to about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(B.K. No. 96 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Beam in the temple of Vīrabhadra.

inscription.p:
This record of about the 13th century A.D. states that Mādarasa had the foot-
prints (of a deity) made in the Jina-mandira.

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(B.K. No. 121 of 1932-33.)
KABBŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken hero-stone near the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription records the grant of nettarugeyi in recognition of the
valour of Asagara Sōmeya-Nyāka who died in a fight at Hāveri. It belongs to the
13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(B.K. No. 137 of 1932-33.)
KARAJGI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Hanumān on the river bank.

inscription.p:
This inscription of about the 13th century A.D. commemorates the death of
Mācheya-Nāyaka probably in a fight at Haveri.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(B.K. No. 142 of 1932-33.)
KITTŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the temple of Jagannātha.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription records the setting up of a hero-stone in memory of
Mādappa who died in a fight. The characters of the record belong to the 13th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(B.K. No. 34 of 1933-34.)
SIRUGUPPI, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the village chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
The record states that a Brahmapuri was established by Daṇṇāyaka Nāgarasa
son of Sādēva. Ayyāvaḷe is mentioned. The characters are of about the 13th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(B.K. No. 108 of 1933-34.)
MŌṬEBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up against the front wall of the temple of Kattala Mallappa.

inscription.p:
This inscription, engraved in characters of the 13th century A.D., is dated in
Saumya, [Chaitra] śu. 10, Thursday. The Śaka year is not given. It states that this (the
slab on which it is written) is the niśidhi stone commemorating the death of
Bāchiseṭṭi, son of Savaṇara Bommiseṭṭi and the disciple of Jinachandradēva.

l.ka: maga bāciseṭṭiya niśidigeya kallu śrī caṁdranā-

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(B.K. No. 150 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone (A-32, back) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription contains in Nāgarī characters the imprecatory
verses of a record. The verses are in Sanskrit. The characters are of the
13th century A.D.

ṣiko dhānyahārī syāt [stena] mu • phalaṁ ka-

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(B.K. No. 25 of 1934-35.)
HOḶI-ĀNAVĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Nandi-pillar near the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
The purpose of this fragmentary and worn out inscription is not clear. It seems
to record a grant of land in the presence of certain Prabhugāvuṇḍus and others.
The characters belong to about the 13th century A.D.

l.note: The rest of the record is

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(B.K. No. 51 of 1934-35.)
MEṆASINHĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the Kalamēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a grant of land as nettarugeyi in memory of Kaliga and
Siriga who died during an attack on Meṇasinakeṟe. The grant was made by Kallaṇṇa
and others in the cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Māgha śu. 15, Monday. The Śaka year is
not given. The characters belong to about the 13th century A.D. Kallaṇṇa is stated
to have been the maneyadhikāri of Kētaṇṇa-Nāyaka.

l.ka: vūraḷivinali mādana kaliga sirigaṁgaḷu kādi bidaḍe kētaṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1934-35.)
NIṬṬŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in the Kallēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription commemorates the death of the son of Kētabōva of Niṭūru in a
fight during an attack on Hebūru. The date of the record is the cyclic year Chitrabhānu
Jyēshṭha śu. Ashṭami, Monday. The Śaka year is not given. The characters belong
to about the 13th Century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1934-35.)
KANVI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up near the village chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record mentions Dēveya-Nāyaka, son of Chavuḍeya-Nāyaka. The characters
belong to about the 13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1934-35.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of a broken Jaina image in the Nagarēs11vara temple.

inscription.p:
This record, which belongs to the 13th Century A.D. states that Rājavve,
daughter of Jēmiseṭṭi, installed (the image of) Kalpa-Jinēndra. Jēmiseṭṭi is stated
to be the disciple of Traividya-Narēndrasena-munipa.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(B.K. No. 92 of 1933-34.)
HONNATTI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar in the temple of Nārāyaṇadēva.

inscription.p:
This record in Nagarī characters of about the 13-14th century A.D. contains
a Sanskrit verse in praise of god Hari.

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(B.K. No. 164 of 1933-34.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pedestal of a Jaina image (C-1) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1232, Sādhāraṇa, Jyēshṭha 6, Monday. The
fortnight is not given. The date probably corresponds to A.D. 1310, May 5,
Monday; f.d.t. ·51.

It is written in Nāgari characters and Sanskrit language and states that a
certain ruler named Krishṇadēva installed the image of a Jaina saint (name lost) who
was the (spiritual) son of Mallidēva of Mūla-saṁgha and Nigam-ānvaya.

l.de:
• • • sake 1232 sādhāra[ṇa*]saṁvatsare [je]ṣṭha | 6 somadine || prasiddhe mūlasaṁghe-
sminnigamānvaya saṁbhavaḥ mallidevātmajo dhīro yaśomātṛ tanūdbhavaḥ vikramā[ditya]-

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(B.K. No. 133 of 1933-34.)
BIJAPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJĀPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pilaster in the Karim-ud-din's-mosque in the Ark-Killah.

inscription.p:
This inscription in Nāgarī characters and Marāṭhi language is dated in Śaka
1242 Raudri corresponding to A.D. 1320-21. It is published in the Memoris of the
Arehaeological Survey of India No. 49, p. 25.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1934-35.)
KUPPĒLŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the Bharamadēva temple.

inscription.p:
This record, dated in Śaka 129[7], Rākshasa, Mārgaśira ba. [13], Monday
records a grant of land to Kalamadēva. The tithi cited corresponds to A.D. 1375
December 21, Friday (not Monday as stated in the record).

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(B.K. No. 44 of 1934-35.)
KARŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Two pieces of a broken slab lying in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1315, Srimukha, Kārtika Śu. 15, Sunday, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1393, October 19, Sunday, f.d.t. ·53.

It states that Dharaṇapa-Nāyaka, son of Nāgadēva-Rāṇeya made a grant of land
for offerings to the goddess [La]kkanāthi (?) dēvi of Harihara. Nāgadēva-Rāṇeya is
stated to be the adhikāri of Maṁgapa-Oḍeya, son of Pādumukhi Bāvappa-Oḍeya.
The grant is stated to have been made at the command and in the presence of (god ?)
Hariharadēva.

l.ka: tti nāguḷada gadde beddalu [ma] • ṟu bījavari [nā]-

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(B.K. No. 157 of 1932-33.)
MALLŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken stone set up in the gate-way of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record registers a gift, probably of money, for burning lights
before a god (name lost). It is engraved in characters of about the 14th century
A.D.

l.note: A few lines before this are completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1934-35.)
BISALHAḶḶI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone set up in Kallēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in the cyclic year Ānanda, Kārtika śu. 12, records the
death of a hero (name lost), who was the son of Bōjaya-Nāyaka. The characters are
of about the 14th century A.D.

l.ka: ·ānāṁda saṁvatsarada kārttika suda 12 si śrī ba • • • • •

• • • ya nāyaka • • • • • bōjayanāyakana
maga tā • • nāga-

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1934-35.)
IṬGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Sati stone on the tank bund.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the death by Sahagamana of Chimmyi, wife a Harikāra
(name lost) in the cyclic year Bhva, Jyēshṭha ba. 9, [Tuesday]. The Śaka year is not
given. The characters belong to the 14th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(B.K. No. 18 of 1934-35.)
HALGĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone on the tank bund.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription states that Narase-Nāyaka of Kāṁkati, made a grant of
land at Haligeṟe for the offerings to a goddess (name lost).

The details of the date preserved in the record, viz., Śaka 132 Jyēshṭha
ba. 30 • • • • • solar eclipse, are insufficient to find out the equivalent.

l.note: Theṟe is written above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(B.K. No. 149 of 1932-33.)
KUḶĒNŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the village gate.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the installation of the deity Rāmāyi (?) in the cyclic year
Pingaḷa, Chaitra ba. 9 Saturday, by Banada-Bōti, wife of Kāse-Bōva of Kuḷuvanūr
(modern Kuḷenūr). The record is engraved in characters of about the 14-15th
century A.D.

l.ka: daḷu piṁgaḷasaṁvatsarada cayitra ba 9 śanivārada dinadali rāma-
yidēvara nilisidaḷu maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī [·ā]

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(B.K. No. 52 of 1933-34.)
KAḌKŌḶ, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing in front of the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This record states that a hero (name lost), who was the son of Amṛi[ta]-Nāyaka
died in a fight on Thursday, Kārtika śu. 8, in the cyclic year Bahudhānya. The
characters belong to the 14-15th century A.D.

l.ka: jitēna labhyatē lakṣumi mrutēnāpi surāṁgana kṣaṇavidhvaṁsini kā[yē] kā ciṁtyā ma-

raṇē raṇē || bahudhā(ṁ)nyasaṁvatsarada kārttika su 8 guruvāradaṁdu miḍi

• • • • • danū

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(B.K. No. 20 of 1934-35.)
HEḌIYĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Sati stone on the bund of Saṇṇakeṟe.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1326, Tāraṇa, Chaitra s11u. [5], Sunday, states that
Māde-Nāyaka installed the mahāsati slab in memory of his mother Malidēvi who died
by the rite of samādhi on account of the death of her husband (name lost).
The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1404, March 16, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1934-35.)
CHALGĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone near the sībārakaṭṭe.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1336, Jaya, Śrāvaṇa śu. Tuesday records the
grant of land given as nettarugeyi to the family of a certain Hobbaya, who died in
a fight in Tiraḷigeṟe. The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1414, August 24
or 31. The given week-day (i.e., Tuesday) occurred twice in the fortnight.

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(B.K. No. 15 of 1934-35.)
CHALGĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying in the threshing floor of Vālīkār Basappa.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1339, Hēmaḷambi, Vaiśākha śu. 1, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1417, April 18. The week-day was Sunday and not Monday
as stated in the record.

It states that when Chavuḍaya, son of Kōṭeya-Nāyaka, died (probably in a fight for
the settlement of the boundary ?) of Mudiyanūr and Tiraḷigeṟe, Hampaṇṇa-Oḍeya, son
of Maṁgappa-Oḍeya, ordered that a grant of land be made to the family of the
deceased. The grant was made at the request of Buḷarasa and others. Buḷḷarasa
was the adhikāri of Hariharadēva.

l.ka: vijavanū tidūtiralu keḷage yeraḍu taḷa bi-
ri bāyināyka ·ā prajegaḷa muṁde koḍahēḷi koṭṭa mā(ṁ)-
nya ·ā cavuḍayana heṁḍiru makkaḷu ·ātana taṁma kē-

dōṣake hōharu maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1933-34.)
BANKAPUR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone leaning against the front wall of the temple of Basavaṇṇa
at Sahazar, a suburb of Bankapur.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription is dated in Śaka 1399 (expired), Durmukhi, Kārtīka
ba. 5, corresponding to A.D. 1476, November 6, Wednesday (not verifiable).

It states that [Bābali]-Nāyakitti, wife of Immaḍi Siṅge-Nāyaka and daughter of
Bhimiseṭṭiya-Māyaṇṇa and Sitamma built a well for the merit of her daughter
Dēmarasi, who died an unnatural death. The major portion of the record which is
damaged extols the merit, in Sanskrit, of making a gift of water (udaka-dāna).

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(B.K. No. 138 of 1932-33.)
KARAJGI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Broken's one built into the front wall of the house of Siddappa Kōṭi.

inscription.p:
This damaged record is dated in Chaitra śu. 10 of (probably) the cyclic year
Vikrama. It seems to state that in Śaka [1]566, Dhātu, the Dēśāyi of Dēvagiri and
Karajige made a gift to god Chenna-Basavēśvaradēva of Kukanūr. The latter date is
wrong because the cyclic and the Śaka years given do not tally.

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(B.K. No. 21 of 1934-35.)
HEḌIYĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone on the bund of Saṇṇakeṟe.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription commemorates the death of Mallagauḍa in the cyclic
year Śubhakṛit, Vaiśākha ba. 5. The village Yātagiri is mentioned. In the absence
of the Śaka year, it is not possible to find out the equivalent of the date. The
characters belong to about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1934-35.)
KADARMAṆḌALGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription seems to refer to the setting up of (the hero-stone
depicting) Timmayya-Nāyaka by Ammajayya-Nāyaka. Timmayya died in a fight
when Kadiramaṇḍalage was attacked.

The record is dated in the cyclic year [Vi]shu, Vaiśākha śu. The Śaka year and
other deatils of the date are not given. The characters belong to about the 15th
century A.D.

l.ka:
kadi[ra]maṁ[ḍa]lageya mō • • varū muttikāduva • • ·aṁmma[ja ya]
nāyaka

tiṁmmayyanū ·aṁbu tāgi vayikuṁṭha prāpti[yādaṁ] ·aṁmmajayyanāyakaru
tiṁmayyana pratiṣṭeya

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(B.K. No. 63 of 1934-35.)
RANĒBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab built into a step of the Hanumān temple near the Veṅkaṭēśvara temple.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription records a grant of land in Ṟaṭehaḷi-sīme by a
person (name lost) of the Jōyisa family. He belonged to the Āśvalyana-sūtra-
Maduveya-suṁka (marriage tax) is mentioned.
The characters belong to about the 15th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(B.K. No. 139 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab (A-19) in the museum.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in Śaka 1422, Siddhārthi, Bhādrapada ba. 3, Friday, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1500, September 3. The week-day, however, was Thursday.

It registers a kaul granted by Gāliba-khān who was holding the ṭhāṇā Māmale
of Mahamadābād and Tāḷikōṭe, to all the Mahānāḍu, headed by Tammaṇaiya-Chaudhiri
of Mahamadābād and others, fixing the amount of taxes to be levied on various
professions, products, livestock, etc. The record contains many Persian words.
