inscription.head:
No. 422.
(B.K. No. 161 of 1932-33.)
NEGḶŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up in the temple of Mallāri.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record of about the 15-16th century A.D., mentions (the village)
Bommanakaṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1934-35.)
BENTŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in front of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a grant of land made to a certain Sōmanātha-Bhaṭṭa by a
Nāyaka (name-lost). The grant was made in Śaka 1433 (current), Pramō[dūta].
Pushya ba. 10 corresponding to A.D. 1510, December 26, Thursday.

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(B.K. No. 13 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Pillar of the maṇṭapa in front of the Yellamma temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1460, Viḷambi, Āshāḍha Śu. 5, Monday, states that
Virayya, son of Daṇḍige Mallayya of Dvagiri built the gateway (of the village)
The details of the date correspond to A.D. 1538, July 1, Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(B.K. No. 160 of 1932-33.)
NARASIPUR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone lying near the temple of Narasiṁha at the village
(which is now deserted).

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 14[7]8, Naḷa, Vaiśākha śu. 14, Sunday, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1556, April 23. The week-day however was Thursday and not
Sunday.

It states that Ellappa-Nāyaka made a gift of a village called Kurubanahaḷḷi alias
Nārasiṁhapura to Toravana-Bhaṭṭa, son of Narasiṁha-Bhaṭṭa of Munavaḷḷi, belonging to
Jāmadagnyavatsa-gōtra, Āśvalāyana-sutra and Rik-śākhā. The donation was made for
the merit of the donor's ancestors. The village was situated in Guttaḷaḷa-vēṇthe in the
Baṅkapura-sīme. The donor also installed the image of god Narasiṁhadēva in the
village and gave some land for the worship and offerings to that god.

vāgi koṭevāgi ·ā grāmadalu nārasiṁhadēvara pratiṣṭheyanumāḍi ·ā dēvara

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(B.K. No. 135 of 1933-34.)
BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Wall of a well called the Nīm-Bāwri, near Gol Gumbaz.

inscription.p:
This record in Nāgarī characters is dated in Śaka 1502, Vikrama, Chaitra śu 1,
Wednesday, corresponding to A.D. 1580, March 16, Wednesday. It begins with a
Sanskrit verse in praise of the holy place Prayāga and records in Marāṭhi the
construction of a well.

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(B.K. No. 41 of 1934-35.)
KADARMAṆḌALGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Garuḍa-pillar set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1503, Vishu, Āshāḍha ba. 14, corresponding to
A.D. 1581 June 29, Thursday (not verifiable).

It records the grant of the village Kaduraviṇḍalige (modern Kadarmaṇḍalgi) for
the worship of the god Hanumantasvāmin on the occasion of Karkāṭaka-saṁkrānti.
This Saṁkrānti occurred on the following Friday. The grant was given
by Keṁgappa-Nāyaka, son of Hanuma-Nāyaka of Saṁtheya Bennur. The village
granted was situated in Ṟaṭṭehaḷḷi Nūruṁbāḍa, which was a sub-division of Chandra-
gutti-vēṇṭhe.

l.ka: tra dhātrīchatraśriyaṁ dadhau [||*] svasti śrī vijayābhyudaya
ke saluva ṟaṭṭehaḷḷi nūṟuṁbāḍada voḷagaṇa ṟaṭa-
haḷiya sīmege saluva beṁnūra staḷada voḷagaṇa
make saluva gru[hā]rāma kṣētra nidhi nikṣēpa jala pāśā-

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1933-34.)
MŪGANŪR, NARAGUND PETHA, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying near the Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p:
This worn out inscription in Nāgarī characters, seems to refer to an agrahāra.
The partially preserved date of the record is Śaka 14 • • Chaitra • • • lunar eclipse

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(B.K. No. 8 of 1932-33.)
ARAḶIHAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying near the temple of Durgavva.

inscription.p:
This short inscription with no date states that this is the mānya field of Sēnabōva
Narasiṁhadēva, son of Tirumalabhaṭṭa of Maḷakhēḍa, who belonged to Haṛita-gōtra
Āpastaṁba-sūtra and Yajuś-śākhā. The characters belong to the 16th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(B.K. No. 110 of 1933-34.)
MŌṬEBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab lying near the village Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This record in characters of the 16th century A.D. states that a certain person
(name lost) built a rest house (dharma-śāle) in Moina-Benndr (Modern Mōṭebennur).
It is dated in the cyclic year Durmukhi, Jyeshha Śu. 6. The Śaka year is not
given.

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(B.K. No. 121 of 1933-34.)
BEḶḶUBBI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i:
Stone built into the parapet wall on the roof of the
Maḷeyappa temple.

inscription.p:
This one-line record of about the 16th Century A.D. states that the place (where
the stone was originally set up) is the threshing floor (kaṇa) of Yakameḷeka.

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(B.K. No. 53 of 1934-35.)
MEṆASINHĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near Kere-kōḍi Siddappa temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift made by Banavāji Sāmarāya to Nāgaṇa-
Bhaṭṭa (son of) Aṇṇi-Bhaṭṭa, the village priest of Vijāpura. The characters belong to
about the 16-17th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(B.K. No. 155 of 1932-33.)
MALLŪR, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in S.No. 180.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1557, Yuva, Phālguṇa śu. 1, Saturday, corres-
ponding regularly to A.D. 1636, February 27, Saturday.

It registers the sale-deed of a land in Malūr (modern Mallūr) executed by Dēvaṇa-
gauḍa, son of Kenchaṇagauḍa of Malūr to Channappagauḍa son of Viraṇṇagauḍa of
Hommaraḍi. The pedigree of Channappagauḍa is given. It is stated that Sāṁtaṇṇa-
gauḍa, son of Paḍḍe Saṁgappagauḍa and an ancestor of Channappagauḍa was
holding the gauḍike of Hommaraḍi-Kaṁpaṇa when Virūpaksharāya was reigning in
Vidyānagara.

l.note: Anotherca is written below this letter.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(B.K. No. 14 of 1933-34.)
BAṄKĀPUR, BANKAPUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up against the front wall of the Dāsāji-maṭha.

inscription.p:
This record is dated in Śaka 1565, Svabhānu, Jyēshṭha śu. 1 corresponding to
A.D. 1643, May 8, Monday.

It states that on account of certain fines imposed on the peasants (Okkalu-makkaḷu)
of the village Bhadrāpura, they vacated the village. Consequently the village became
deserted and so Dēśayi Buḷḷappa, the gauḍa of Bhadrāpura brought them back and
rehabilitated them. For this act, he was granted a gift of land by the king.

l.ka: ṣṭa śudha 1 dallū | bhadrāpurada haḷemokhāsiya ·ā grāmada vakkalu-
makkaḷa kaiya daṁḍava koṁḍa saṁma[ṁ*]dha yallā biṭṭu hōgi ·ūru haḷā-
gi yiralāgi ·ā bhadrāpurada gauḍanādaṁthā baṁkāpurada sīmeya
. da saṁmaṁdha ·ā grāmada gauḍanadaṁthā dēśāyi buḷappage ·ārasu-
. bhu koṭa manyada hollavu makkaḷu makkaḷu para[ṁ*]para ·eṁdu koṭa-
du sukadalli yihadu yeṁdu koṭṭa manye • • ||

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(B.K. No. 16 of 1934-35.)
GŌḌIHĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Slab set up in the Garbhagṛiha of the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 159[4], Naḷa, [Pushya] śu. 12. The Saka
year given coincided with the cyclic year Prabhava and not Naḷa. In the year
Naḷa, the details correspond to A.D. 1677, January 12, Friday.

It records the grant of the village Gāḍehaḷu, to the Vaishṇava pontiff Satyādhiśa-
śripāda, disciple of Satyabhinava-tīrtha who was the disciple of Satyanātha-Tītha. The
grant was made by Lingappa-gauḍa, the Deśayi of Guttāḷal, for the worship of god
Srī Ramachandra in the maṭha of the donee.

It may be noted that the date given in the record is too early for the characters
in which it is engraved. The palaeography of the record would indicate that it is
probably a later copy.

l.note: Two strokes are engraved at the beginning of

l.ka: baḷi koṭu yidēve | yī maṭhada śrī-
du barasi koṭa dāna[saṁsthe] maṁgaḷa ma-

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(B.K. No. 4 of 1932-33.)
AGAḌI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone pedestal of a pillar in the temple of Venkaṭēśa.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1600, Kāḷayukta, Kārtika śu. 12, Thursday,
corresponding to 1678 A.D., October 17, Thursday, f.d.t. ·03. The grant portion is
very badly damaged. Mentions a certain Dēśāyi (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1934-35.)
BEḶKĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in S. No. 88.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1604 (current), Durmati, Phālguna śu. 15,
Tuesday, lunar eclipse. The week-day is wrong. The tithi given corresponds to A.D.
1682, February 11, which was a Saturday. There was a lunar eclipse on this day.

It records a grant of land made by Lingappa-gauḍa, son of Hanumanta-gauḍa and
the grandson of Basavaṇṇa-gauḍa, the Desayi of Gutalal (modern Guttal in Haveri
Taluk) to Narahari-Bhatta, son of Bhikaṇa-Bhaṭṭa and the grandson of Giri-Bhaṭṭa.
The donee belonged to the Kātyāyana-sūtra, Sukla-yajuś-śākhā and Kauṇḍinya-gōtra
and the donor belonged to the same sūtra and śākhā and Mōnabhargava-gōtra.

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(B.K. No. 166 of 1933-34.)
BIJJARGI, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.i: Village gate-way near the Chāvaḍi.

inscription.p:
This damaged record in Nāgarī characters is dated in Śaka 1677, Yuva, Chaitra
śu. Pratīpadā, corresponding to A.D. 1755, March 13, Thursday.
It records in Mārāṭhi, the construction of a gate in the village Bījaragi.

l.de: • • 167[7] yīvā nāma saṁvachara caiṁtra suda pratīpadā
te divasīsa | masvasābhaṁ | na | cīka pāṭīla kulaka-
rṇī • na bījaragī yāna gāvata daravājā bāṁdala • •

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1933-34.)
BAMMANAKAṬṬI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the Hoṇḍa.

inscription.p:
The stone denoting the boundary of the field of Limgappa, son of Sūrappa, states
that it was erected in the cyclic year Tāraṇa. Sūrappa seems to have been the
accountant (Sānubhōga) of ommanakaṭṭi. The characters belong to the 7th century
A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(B.K. No. 158 of 1932-33.)
MAṆṬAGAṆI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying near the temple of Īśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription states that Male-Nāyaka, son of Maṅge-Nāyaka, the mādāra of
Maṇṭagaṇī and Male-Nāyaka, son of Maḷe Nāyaka of Yettinahaḷḷi, together gave half
of the mādārike of Maṇṭagaṇi to Girige-Nāyaka and Gaṅga and Malla, the sons of
Chandeya-Nāyaka of Kaḷasūr.

The date of this record is lost. It is written in characters of about the 17th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1933-34.)
CHIKKA-NARGUNDA, NARAGUND PETHA, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the Basavaṇṇa temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record belonging to the 17th century A.D. is dated in the cyclic
year Rudhirōdgāri, Pushya ba. 14 and records a grant made to a certain Buḷḷappa, by
Basaṇṇa-Nāyaka, son of Bommaṇṇa-Nāyaka of Naṟugunda. The details of the grant
are lost.

l.ka: ṇa • • • buḷapagaḷige koṭa dharmaśāśanada paṭheya krama-

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(B.K. No. 105 of 1933-34.)
KAJRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the temple of Kallēśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Nandana, Māgha, ba. 30, Monday
solar eclipse. The Śaka year is not given. The characters belong to about the 17th
century A.D.

It records that Hiriya-Vīraṇṇa, the Mahāprabhu of Homnavatti and others made
a grant of land to Chauḍappa, son of Saṁkapayya of Navile and belonging to Agastya-
gōtra.

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(B.K. No. 107 of 1933-34.)
MOṬEBENNŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Fragment of a slab standing in the Public Works Department bungalow.

inscription.p:
This fragmentary record registers a grant of land for the worship of a god (name
ost).

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(B.K. No. 131 of 1932-33.)
KAṆAVAḶḶI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone built into the front wall of the temple of Nīlakaṇṭhēśvara.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1638, Durmukhi, Mārgaśira śu. 6, Sunday, registers
a grant of land made by Bahadūra Hānumanta-gauḍa, the Dēśāyi of Guttaḷa, at the time
of the car-festival of Mailāraliṅga. The grant was made for the merit of the donor's
forefathers. The details of the given date correspond to A.D. 1716, November 9,
Friday (not Sunday as stated in the record).

l.ka: re vibhuti rudrākṣe tappida hage vi[ṣṇu]-

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1934-35.)
KUPPĒLŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near Maṭhada hola.

inscription.p:
This inscription, the purport of which is not clear is dated in Śāka 1652, Naḷa,
Māgha śu. 14. The date is wrong. Śaka 1652 coincided with the cyclic year Sādhāraṇa
and the cyclic year Naḷa coincided with Śaka 1658. In these two years the given
tithi corresponds to A.D. 1730, February 22, Thursday and A.D. 1737, February 3,
Thursday, respectively.

At the top of this stone are engraved some indistinct letters and a certain
Marudēva seems to have been mentioned.

l.note: These lines are engraved at the top of the

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone set up in the Viraktamaṭha.

inscription.p:
This inscription dated in Śaka 1681, Pramāthi, Jyēshṭha ba. 10 corresponding to
A.D. 1759 June 20, Wednesday, records the foundation of the Virakta-maṭha of
Huchcha-Chanavirappa, the charamūrti (disciple ?) of Huchcha-Basavaliṅgasvāmi.

l.note: Mistake forsthāpane. Scribal errors are numerous.

edition.l: maṭada stapani Mistake for sthāpane. Scribal errors are numerous.©

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1933-34.)
SŌMĀPUR, NARGUND PETHA, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Beam in the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This inscription which is in Nāgarī characters and Sanskrit language records,
the installation of god Hanumān at Sōmāpura by a certain Dēvarāya in Śaka 1690,
Sarvadhāri, Vaiśākha śu. Trayōdaśi, Friday corresponding to A.D. 1768 April 29, Friday.

l.de: śrī hanūmate namaḥ || sarvadhāriṇi vasaṁtamādhave pū-
sarvadhārī saṁvatsara vaiśākha śuddha trayodaśī śukra-

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(B.K. No. 56 of 1933-34.)
KEṄGOṆḌA, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the garden to the east of the village.

inscription.p:
This record dated in Śaka 1702, Śārvari, Vaiśākha ba. 10 corresponding to
May 28, Sunday (not verifiable), states that Sāhadara Hanumantagauḍa, the Dēśāyi of
Guttaḷal bestowed the office of the Accountant (Śānubhōvike) of Keṁgonḍa situated in
the Mahal of Kāginele on a person (not named). A naiar of 300 varāhas was taken
from him for granting the privilege.

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(B.K. No. 136 of 1932-33.)
KARAJGI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in Kamatada Hola on the roa to Kōḷūr.

inscription.p:
This record in Nāgarī characters and Kannaḍa language denotes the land granted
by Buḷapa-Dēsāyi, to Subrahmaṇyāchārya, son of Bāḷāchārya and grandson of
Galgali Mudagallāchārya. It belongs to about the 18th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(B.K. No. 60 of 1933-34.)
NIRALGI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
The stone engraved in characters of about the 18th century A.D. is a
boundary stone called Vāmanamudre-kallu. It states that it marks the boundary
of Gutaḷala. The Dēśāyi of Lakshmēśara erected it.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(B.K. No. 81 of 1933-34.)
CHANDĀPUR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in front of the house of Hanumanta Gauḍa Pāṭīl.

inscription.p:
This record in characters of the 18th century A.D. states that a land at Chenda-
pura was granted as Juḍimānya to Jakkaṇṇa son of Pūjāri Puṭṭaṇṇa. The grant
was made by Bāhadūra Chikkapa-gauḍa, the Dēśāyi of Guttaḷa, at the request of the
donee, under certain conditions (specified in the record).

The date of the record is the cyclic year Dhātṛi, Kārtika, śu. 13. The Śaka
year is not given.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(B.K. No. 48 of 1932-33.)
GUTTAL, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying on the tank bund about 2 miles from the village.

inscription.p:
This inscription in characters of about the 18-19th century A.D. is a boundary-
stone of the field which was the mānya for the gauḍike of Guttaḷal, given to Dēśāyi
Hanumantagauḍa of Guttaḷal who belonged to Mōnabhārgava-gōtra Kātyāyana-sūtra
and Sukla-Yajuś-śākhā.

l.ka: ttagauḍarā kasabe guttaḷala gauḍiki mūṁnye mē-

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(B.K. No. 49 of 1932-33.)
GUTTAL, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone lying on the tank bund about 2 miles from the village.

inscription.p: This record is a copy of the previous one (No. 452).

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1932-33.)
AGAḌI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing in S.No. 276.

inscription.p:
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1726, Raktākshi, Adhika-Chaitra śu. 15, corres-
ponding to A.D. 1804, March 26 Monday (not verifiable). It records that Sivarāya-
Jōyisa, son of Jayarāma-Sāstri and the Sadara-Jōyisa of Hāvanūr was granted a piece of
land by Bahadara Liṅgaṇṇa-gauḍa grandson of Dēsāyi Bahadara Liṅgappa-gauḍa
of Guttaḷala, for the beautitude of his ancestors.

l.ka: ḷala bahadara liṁgappagauḍara pautrarāda bahadara ha-

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(B.K. No. 26 of 1932-33.)
DĒVAGERI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the Kallumaṇṭapa.

inscription.p:
This inscription in Nāgarī characters of about the 19th century A.D. mentions
Dyāvaṇa-Bhaṭa (Dēvaṇṇa Bhaṭṭa) of Māgaṇagēri belonging to Kauṇḍinya-gōtra,
Kātyāyana-sūtra and Sukla-Yajuś-śākhā.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(B.K. No. 100 of 1932-33.)
HĀVĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in the temple of Venkaṭēśvara in the agrahāra.

inscription.p:
This inscription written in characters of about the 19th century A.D., seems to
record the installation of the (image) of god Srīnivāsa. The record is very
indistinct and illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(B.K. No. 29 of 1933-34.)
KĀGINELLI, HIREKERUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone in front of the temple of Basavaṇṇa.

inscription.p:
This record in Nāgarī characters indicates the property of a certain Bhīmāchārya
son of Kaṭṭi Srinivāsachārya. The characters are of about the 19th century A.D.

l.note:
Below the stone is carved in
a water pitcher in the left. Such a stone is generally called Vāmana-Mudre kallu.

edition.p: 1. The rest of the record is illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(B.K. No. 17 of 1934-35.)
GŌḌIHĀḶ, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone on Maṭhada-kaṇa.

inscription.p:
This inscription, the date of which is lost, is engraved in late characters. It
states that the Vaishṇava pontiff Satyabōdha-Śrīpāda-oḍeya, disciple of Satyapriya-
Śripāda-Oḍeya, built a maṭha in the village Gōḍihaḷu and placed it in charge of
Kambāḷayya, son of Koṇḍara Rudrappa. The characters belong to the 19th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(B.K. No. 42 of 1934-35.)
KADARMAṆḌALGI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Hero-stone standing in front of the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p: This badly damaged record in late characters mentions Huligere.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1934-35.)
TUMMINAKAṬṬI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This damaged record in late characters is dated in Śaka • • • 17, Bhādrapada ba.
1[2]. The cyclic year also is lost. It records a grant of land to a Vaishṇava pontiff
(name lost) for worship and offerings to god Rāmachandradēva. The river
Tuṅgabhadra is mentioned.

l.ka: śrī gaṇādhipatayē nama[ḥ |] nirvighnamastu | śrī gurubhyōṁna-

matparamahaṁ-
• • • • • dānapaṭṭe || yiti śrī śrī śrī śrī ślōka

taṁ padaṁ || 2 || svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ paradattāpahārēṇa svada
ttaṁ viṣṭhalaṁ bhavētu || 3 ||

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(B.K. No. 115 of 1933-34.)
NŪKĀPUR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone standing near the temple of Hanumān.

inscription.p:
This inscription in Nāgarī characters is almost completely effaced except for a
few words that are faintly visible.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(B.K. No. 19 of 1934-35.)
HALGĒRI, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.i: Stone near the Hanumān temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the syllables ṇā and prā in different combinations
which formed probably a mystic yantra.
